[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20250715085802..png) Graduation Ceremony 2025 - A Celebration of Academic Excellence Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, held its prestigious Graduation Ceremony 2025 on 14th July, honouring graduates for their academic achievements and holistic development. The convocation ceremony, held in the presence of Ms Sheeba Mumtaz C K, Assistant Director, Department of Social Justice, Govt of Kerala, Dr Jos T Puthur, Professor, Dept of Botany, Director of IQAC, University of Calicut, Sr Saly Paul, Provincial Superior and Manager, CMC Nirmala Province, Thrissur, Principal Dr. Sr. Beena Jose, Dr Vimala M, Controller of Examinations; saw enthusiastic participation from graduates, faculty, and parents, making it a proud and memorable day for all.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/WhatsApp%20Image%202025-07-15%20at%208_20250715085802.jpeg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346.png) World Jackfruit Day Celebrated with Culinary Flair The Department of Home Science at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in collaboration with Malayala Manorama and Ayur Jack Farm, celebrated World Jackfruit Day on 4th July 2025 at the Silver Jubilee Hall. The event featured a recipe competition and demonstration class, emphasizing the culinary and nutritional significance of jackfruit. Mr. A Jeevankumar (Coordinating Editor, Malayala Manorama) delivered the presidential address, and Mr. Varghese Tharakan (Owner, Ayurjack Farm) served as the chief guest. Dr. Sr. Beena Jose, Principal, inaugurated the event, which brought together students, faculty, and food enthusiasts in a delightful tribute to the versatile fruit.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML7184_20250704122905.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/540x346_20250625115748..png) Life Science Summit 2025 - Day 1: Echoes of Life Begins with Insightful Inauguration and Expert Talks The Department of Zoology, in collaboration with the Bhoomithrasena Club, inaugurated the Life Science Summit 2025–26 on 24th June 2025 at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur. Day 1 commenced with an official inauguration followed by enlightening sessions by Dr. V. S. Vijayan (Chairman, Salim Ali Foundation) on Biodiversity Conservation and Sustainable Development and Dr. Lalitha Vijayan (Honorary Director, Salim Ali Foundation) who presented a case study on Vellangallur Panchayat as a Model for Sustainable Development. The day concluded with vibrant paper presentations by students and researchers on diverse themes in life sciences.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML6967_20250625115748.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/540x346%20\(1\)_20250625115855..png) Life Science Summit 2025 - Day 2: Marine Biodiversity, Biospheres and Student Research Take the Spotlight The second day of the Life Science Summit 2025–26, held on 25th June 2025, at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, brought engaging sessions and research to the forefront. Dr. Jayaraj K. A. (Associate Professor, Pondicherry University, Andaman Campus) delivered a compelling talk on Marine Biodiversity Threats and Conservation, followed by Mrs. Manjusha U. on Closed Biosphere Terrariums. The summit concluded with another round of student paper presentations, showcasing a variety of research in biodiversity, conservation, cancer biology, and more—reflecting academic curiosity and eco-conscious vision.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML6985_20250625115855.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/images/newsDefault.jpg) Vimala College Shines in India Today - MDRA Best College Survey 2025 Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, has once again made its mark nationally in the India Today – MDRA Best College Survey 2025. The college secured impressive ranks in multiple streams: 61st in Arts 41st in Science 40th in Commerce 26th in MSW (Master of Social Work) Notably, the MSW programme has also been ranked 8th in India under the category of Best Value for Money Programmes, highlighting the college's commitment to accessible, quality education. These rankings are a testament to Vimala’s academic excellence, student-focused initiatives, and holistic development.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/india%20today%20-mdra_20250623115923.png)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/540x346_20250621085119..png) Vimala College Celebrates International Yoga Day 2025 In observance of International Yoga Day 2025, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, organized a peaceful and energizing yoga session under the guidance of the Department of Physical Education. Students from across departments gathered in unity to perform Surya Namaskaram (Sun Salutation) and Chandra Namaskaram (Moon Salutation), embracing the essence of yoga as a path to holistic wellness. The event emphasized mindfulness, physical harmony and spiritual awareness, reflecting the college’s dedication to nurturing the overall well-being of its student community.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML6909_20250621085119.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/images/newsDefault.jpg) VIMALAARAMBH 2025 - Student Induction Programme for First Year UG & PG Students Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur warmly welcomed its first-year UG and PG students through VIMALAARAMBH 2025, a student induction programme organized by the IQAC on 20th June 2025 at 9:30 AM in the College Auditorium. The event began with an inspiring inaugural session attended by students, parents, and faculty. It served as a meaningful orientation introducing the college’s vision, values, academic structure, and campus culture. The presence of the Principal Dr. Sr Beena Jose, Vice Principals Dr. Malini K A and Sr Ani Davis K, added grace and guidance to the day’s proceedings. The event set a strong foundation for a vibrant and enriching college life.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML6762_20250620030945.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/540x346%20\(1\)_20250620031354..png) Yoga Competition in Celebration of International Yoga Day 2025 In connection with International Yoga Day 2025, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur organized a vibrant department-wise Yoga competition on campus. The event, coordinated by the Department of Physical Education, aimed to promote the importance of health, flexibility, and mindfulness among students through the art of yoga. Participants from various departments showcased rhythmic, synchronized yoga performances, highlighting both physical fitness and team coordination. The competition encouraged unity, discipline, and a deeper understanding of yoga’s holistic benefits. Winners were awarded medals and certificates of appreciation.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML6873_20250620031354.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/540x346.png) ARSDI Foundation Day Celebrated with Awareness Session on Dementia Care Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, observed ARSDI Foundation Day with an insightful session on Home-Based Dementia Care on 17th June 2025. The event was graced by Prof. K.S. Shaji M.D, Director (Research), State Resource Centre for Older People, Kerala University of Health Sciences, as the Chief Guest and Resource Person. Organized in association with dementia care experts, the session emphasized the need for compassionate, home-centered dementia support, drawing participation from faculty, students, and healthcare professionals.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/IMG_5649_20250619093948.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/540x346%20\(1\).png) Buchgeist: The Spirit of Books - Celebrated by Department of English The PG and Research Department of English, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, successfully hosted “Buchgeist: The Spirit of Books” on 18th June 2025. Students and faculty engaged in meaningful discussions celebrating literature, creativity, and the timeless spirit of books.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML6562_20250619095503.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/540x346_20250619045820..png) Vaayana 2025 - Celebrating the Joy of Reading The Department of Malayalam and the Library Club of Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, jointly organized Vaayana 2025, a two-day reading festival on June 19 and 20, 2025. Vishnu Prasad, poet and novelist, who inspired students through his literary journey. The festival witnessed enthusiastic participation from students and faculty, reinforcing the timeless value of books in academic and personal growth.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML6700_20250619045820.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/Copy%20of%2013062025%20\(1\).png) Awareness Session on Drug Abuse and Social Responsibility An awareness session on “Drug Abuse, Empathy and Social Responsibility” organised by the Post Graduate Department of Commerce and Research for the second year Bcom students was conducted by Shri Jose C.C, trustee of TRE Palliative Care, Cheroor, Thrissur, on the 13th of June 2025 as part of the observance of the International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking. Known for his unique style “Pattum Parachilum”, he engaged students through songs and moving stories that highlighted the impact of drug abuse and the importance of empathy. Students also contributed essential items like soaps and sanitary napkins for POCSO victims, reinforcing the session’s message of compassion and social responsibility.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/WhatsApp%20Image%202025-06-13%20at%2014_20250613054825.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/WhatsApp%20Image%202025-06-12%20at%2012.26.01_2f305255.jpg) CGPT Organizes LinkedIn Workshop for UG Students The Centre for Graduate Placement and Training (CGPT) at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, conducted a LinkedIn Workshop on 11th June 2025 for all undergraduate students. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/Awards.jpg) Vimala Students Top Value Education Examination 2025 Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, proudly celebrates the achievement of Nayana Suresh and Manya M. from B.Sc. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/DSC_8383.jpg) State-Level Recognition for Bhoomithrasena Club and Faculty Excellence Vimala College proudly received the State-Level Best Bhoomithrasena Club Award from the Directorate of Environment and Climate Change, Government of Kerala, for the academic year 2024–2025. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/2O3A5979.jpg) Environment Day 2025 Celebrated with Tree Planting and Awareness Drives Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, marked World Environment Day 2025 with a series of impactful programmes organized by NSS Units 43 & 120, in association with the Departments of Physics and Sociology. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/2O3A6119.jpg) Beautification Project Launched under Garbage-Free Kerala Initiative Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in collaboration with NSS Units 43 & 120, officially launched the “Beautification of College Road” under the ‘Garbage-Free Kerala’ Initiative on June 5, 2025. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/THUMB%20LOGO.png) National Level Essay Contest on Kerala's Economic Crisis The Post Graduate Department of Economics, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, organized a National Level Essay Contest sponsored by South Indian Bank, Thrissur, focusing on Kerala’s ongoing economic crisis. Despite receiving 18 entries from students across India, the jury found none met the required conceptual and analytical standards. As a result, no top prizes were awarded; instead, three participants - Ardra K Baburaj (Vimala College), Nidhi S. (CUSAT), and Bonny John (Symbiosis University) - were recognized with consolation prizes of ₹10,000 each for their efforts.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/news/view-pdf/nationallevelessaycontest)

[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/WhatsApp%20Image%202025-03-03%20at%201.59.03%20PM%20\(1\)_cr.jpg) Field Visit to Kerala Fire & Rescue Services Academy The PG Department of Sociology, in association with the Fire Force Academy, organized a field visit to the Kerala Fire and Rescue Services Academy, Thrissur, as part of the Disaster Management multidisciplinary course. Conducted by Officers Radhakrishnan, Aswin, and Vishnu, the session provided valuable insights into disaster preparedness, emergency response, and rescue operations. Students participated in hands-on training, including CPR and first aid demonstrations, enhancing their confidence in handling emergency situations. The visit emphasized the importance of teamwork, coordination, and communication in disaster management. This enriching experience highlighted the need for disaster awareness and preparedness, paving the way for future collaborations with the Fire Force Academy.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/WhatsApp%20Image%202025-03-03%20at%201_20250303023152.jpg)

[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20260116113627..jpg) Magnus 2k26: Vimala College Celebrates Golden Jubilee with Grand Management Fest The PG Department of Commerce and Research at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, successfully hosted Magnus 2k26, a national-level management fest, to commemorate its Golden Jubilee year. This year’s flagship event, themed "Business Track," featured a diverse range of management competitions including the Best Management Team, HR Game, Marketing Game, and Business Quiz. Beyond corporate strategy, the fest also showcased artistic talents through a Non-Management track, highlighting events like Group Dance, Photography, and Reel Making. The event brought together students from various institutions across the country, fostering leadership, creativity, and holistic development in a competitive yet collaborative environment.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML3890_20260116113627.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20260116021802..jpg) Global Academic Laurels: Dr. Vanaja V.S. at the 19th World Sanskrit Conference, Nepal International Achievement by Faculty (Abroad) - Dr. Vanaja VS has been invited for 19th WORLD SANSKRIT CONFERENCE at Kathmandu, Nepal, a prestigious global Sanskrit gathering from 26th to 30th June 2025 organized by Nepal Sanskrit University with the financial assistance from Central Sanskrit University, New Delhi. Dr. Vanaja VS presented a Research Paper on "Buddha's Perception on Happiness and Peace". - The Jvālā Malayalam translation of the famous Sanskrit drama Dūtaghaṭotkacam of Mahākavi Bhāsa by Dr. Vanaja V. S. and Dr. Reshma Ramachandran P was formally released by Prof. P. C. Muraleemadhavan and presented to Prof. Haridath Sharma on 28th June 2025 in Nepal. Dr. Vanaja VS attended Panel discussion with eminent scholars and researchers in Sanskrit language, literature, philosophy and allied disciplines. She has also visited many world heritage places at Nepal. - Dr. Vanaja VS has been selected as a Member from India in International Association of Sanskrit Studies as part of World Sanskrit Conference  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/WhatsApp%20Image%202026-01-16%20at%202_20260116021802.jpeg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20260115102456..jpg) Python Training Success at Vimala College Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, recently hosted a successful two-day hands-on training program on "Python for Data Analysis" on January 15 and 16, 2026. Organised by the Post Graduate & Research Department of Statistics in collaboration with the UGC Centre for Women’s Studies, the workshop provided participants with practical expertise in modern data tools. Led by AI and ML mentor Namitha R, the sessions covered a comprehensive curriculum including Jupyter notebooks, Pandas for data manipulation, and advanced visualization libraries like Matplotlib and Seaborn. This initiative highlights the department’s commitment to academic excellence following its recent elevation to a Research Department under Calicut University.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML3550_20260115103103.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20260114110312..jpg) Vimala College Shines at Calicut University Intercollegiate Roller Skating Championship 2025-26 Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, delivered an outstanding performance at the Calicut University Intercollegiate Roller Skating Championship 2025–26. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20260112112515..jpg) MESMER 2026: A Spectacular Fashion Showcase by B.Sc. Textiles & Fashion Technology Students The Department of Home Science organized a fashion show MESMER 2026 which was done as a part of the final presentation of the third year B.Sc. Textiles and Fashion Technology students. The inauguration was done by Nevin Cappresious, Fashion Choreographer and Pageant Trainer. Dr. Thomas Ruby Mariamma, (Head, Department of Home Science) delivered the welcome address. The presidential address was conveyed by Dr. Sr. Beena Jose, Principal of Vimala College Thrissur.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML2438_20260112112515.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20260107115915..jpg) Vimala College Hosts International Seminar on Biodiversity and Conservation On January 7th and 8th, 2026, the Department of Zoology at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in association with the UGC Centre for Women’s Studies and DST Curie, hosted a landmark International Seminar titled "Reversing Extinction, Restoring Hope". The event brought together global experts to discuss critical thrust areas, including biodiversity, environmental biology, and toxicology. +4 The seminar featured prominent speakers such as Dr. K.V. Sankaran, who discussed invasive alien species, and Ingrid Coetzee from ICLEI Africa, who spoke on protecting urban biodiversity. Other sessions covered topics ranging from amphibian extinction to ecosystem conservation for disaster risk reduction. The event also provided a platform for UG/PG students and research scholars to present original research, with selected abstracts included in an ISBN-certified book published by the college.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML2208_20260107115915.jpg)

[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251219092228..jpg) The Function of Music: Singing and Dialogues The Department of Social Work, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, successfully organized a one-day programme titled “The Function of Music: Singing and Dialogues” on 18 December 2025 at the Silver Jubilee Seminar Hall. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251216124713..jpg) International Seminar on Sustainable Futures: Ecology, Environment and Eco-Tourism The Post Graduate and Research Department of Sociology, Vimala College (Autonomous), successfully concluded its One Day International Seminar on "Ecology, Environment and Eco-Tourism in the Modern Context" today, December 16, 2025. The event provided a dynamic platform for critical discussion, featuring distinguished resource persons, including Dr. Fency Sivadasan (Director, Sarawak Tourism Board, Malaysia), Dr. T V Sajeev (Chief Scientist, KFRI), and Mr. K Sethu Raman IPS (Director, Kerala Police Academy). Sessions covered vital topics such as Responsible Tourism, indigenous conservation practices, and reflections on climate change field work. The seminar also hosted parallel paper presentations from research scholars and faculty, reinforcing Vimala College's commitment to fostering international academic exchange and environmental stewardship.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML0300_20251216124713.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251215105637..jpg) Guest Lecture on Keralas Folk Traditions and Narrative Culture Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, organised a guest lecture as part of its academic extension activities, focusing on Kerala’s rich folk traditions and narrative heritage. The session was led by Dr. Merin Joy, who offered insightful perspectives on indigenous cultural expressions and storytelling practices in Kerala. The lecture encouraged students to engage critically with local knowledge systems and cultural history, fostering a deeper appreciation of regional traditions.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML0274_20251215105637.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251212113321..jpg) Hands-on Training in LED Bulb Making Ignites Interest at Vimala College The PG and Research Department of Physics at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, successfully organized a "Hands-on Training in LED Bulb Making" event on December 12, 2025. The training, focusing on Affordable and Clean Energy , was held at the Physics Department Lab. The session was led by Mr. Majeendran Kunnath, Instructor in the Department of Mechatronics Engineering at Jyothi Engineering College, Thrissur. The practical training aimed to equip participants with skills in making LED bulbs, promoting sustainable and energy-efficient practices. The event saw the patronage of Prof. Dr. Sr. Beena Jose (Principal) , Prof. Dr. Malini KA (Vice-Principal) , and Dr. Mini Krishna K (Head of the Department).  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML0237_20251212113321.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251210090109..jpg) LinkedIn Workshop for Faculty Members Held as Part of Emantra 2025 The Department of Computer Science, in association with the Faculty Enrichment Committee, organized a LinkedIn Workshop for faculty members on 9th December 2025 at Marian Hall as part of the Computer Literacy Day Celebrations – Emantra 2025. Mr. Deepak Mukundan Pallyiyil, Communicative English and Soft Skill Trainer, served as the resource person for the session. The workshop covered key areas such as LinkedIn profile optimisation, post creation, self-marketing, and personal branding. Faculty members actively participated, gaining practical insights into enhancing their professional presence on LinkedIn.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML0099_20251210090109.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251209123712..jpg) Inauguration of Five New Research Centres at Vimala College Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, proudly inaugurated five newly approved Research Centres in Statistics, Sociology, Botany, Zoology, and Chemistry on 9 December 2025 at Marian Hall. Principal Prof. Dr. Sr. Beena Jose and Dr. T. V. Sajeev, Chief Scientist, KFRI, also addressed the gathering.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML0044_20251209123712.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/Untitled%20design.jpg) Psephological Survey Conducted by the PG & Research Department of Sociology The PG and Research Department of Sociology, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, conducted a Psephological Survey from 5th to 9th December 2025 at the 7th Ward of the Thrissur Municipal Corporation. The two-day academic field initiative enabled students to gain hands-on exposure to social research, data collection, and community engagement. Guided by faculty and student coordinators, the survey aimed to deepen understanding of voting behaviour, civic awareness, and local governance patterns.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/WhatsApp%20Image%202025-12-08%20at%203_20251208032813.jpeg)

Antony Memorial Annual Lecture Series, organized an insightful lecture led by Mr. Marcos V. P., noted professor, scholar, and former Malayalam Department faculty at Union Christian College, Aluva. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251205114826..jpg) Video Release on Disability Awareness Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, organized a meaningful Disability Awareness initiative through the release of a special video created by the Department of Social Work. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251204085536..jpg) Community Living Camp at Aashrayam Orchard The PG Department of Psychology, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur conducted a three-day Community Living Camp at Aashrayam Orchard, Kollengode, Palakkad District from 28 to 30 November 2025, in collaboration with Aashrayam Rural Development Society, Kollengode. The camp aimed to provide students with immersive community-based learning experiences, focusing on social awareness, teamwork, and personal growth under the guidance of staff coordinators Dr. Sinto P Anto (HOD), Anusree M R, and Renuka Raveendran, with Principal Dr. Sr Beena Jose offering overall support.​  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/WhatsApp%20Image%202025-12-04%20at%208_20251204085536.jpeg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251204014100..jpg) Hands-on Training in Molecular Techniques The Department of Botany, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur organized a “Hands-on Training in Molecular Techniques” in association with the Travancore Institute for Bioscience Research (TIBR), Thiruvananthapuram. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251203112100..jpg) Seminar on Global Opportunities at the University of Birmingham The International Student Cell of Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in association with Michaels Wings Global for International Scholarships, organized a seminar titled “Dream, Apply, Achieve: Opportunities at the University of Birmingham” on 03 December 2025 at Marian Hall. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346%20\(1\)_20251203112354..jpg) Ezuthola 2025 - Literary Presentation & Meet-the-Author Session The Department of Malayalam, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, organized Ezuthola 2025, a special literary presentation and interactive session on 3 December 2025 at Silver Jubilee Hall. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346%20\(1\)_20251124124211..jpg) MOU Signing & Expert Talk on Indian Capital Market at Vimala College Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur hosted a special event on November 24th, 2025, featuring an MOU signing ceremony and an expert talk by Dr. V. K. Vijayakumar, Chief Investment Strategist at Geojit Financial Services. The session, titled "Indian Capital Market: Emerging Trends," provided students and faculty with valuable insights into market dynamics and investment strategies. The programme was held at Marian Hall, with the presence of Principal Dr. Sr. Beena Jose, HOD Dr. Sitara V. Attokkaran, and Faculty Coordinator Dr. Liji Maliakkel.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML8137_20251124124211.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251124015315..jpg) Expert Session on Social Case Work by Dr. Asha P Rao On November 24th, 2025, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur organized an insightful session on "An Introduction to Social Case Work" led by Dr. Asha P Rao, Former Head and Associate Professor, Department of Social Work. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346%20\(1\)_20251121090812..jpg) Weightlifting Triumph: Vimala College Wins Best Lifter Award Best Lifter Award Secured by Vimala College Student in Calicut University Weightlifting A spectacular display of strength! [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251121124938..jpg) Aurora Litterarum - Association Inauguration 2025 PG & Research Department of English Inaugurates 'Aurora Litterarum' Association The PG & Research Department of English, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, successfully inaugurated its Association, Aurora Litterarum – The Dawn of Letters, on November 21, 2025, at 10.30 am in Marian Hall. The inauguration was graced by Prof. (Dr.) Suja Mathew, Professor of English at Sri C. Achutha Menon Govt. College, Thrissur, who was the Chief Guest. Following the ceremony, a special talk was held on the topic: "Circles without Exit: The Fictional Oeuvre of Laszlo Krasznahorkai".  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML8068_20251121124939.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251121025911..jpg) VISMAYAM FEST 2025: Grand Intercollegiate Arts & Innovation Showcase VISMAYAM FEST 2025 Concludes: A Two-Day Celebration of Arts and Innovation Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, successfully concluded VISMAYAM FEST 2025, a spectacular two-day intercollegiate arts and cultural fest held on November 20-21, 2025. The event was a collaborative effort by various departments, including Economics, Physics, Sociology, Mathematics, and Malayalam, and brought together a vibrant array of competitions and creative showcases. Students from colleges across Kerala participated in events celebrating innovation, talent, and artistic expression through quizzes, cultural performances, photography, fashion shows, and much more, cementing VISMAYAM 2025 as a major event on the college calendar.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML7271%20copy_20251121025911.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251119050606..jpg) International Seminar on Degrading Ecosystems: Challenges, Consequences and Conservation Strategies The Department of Botany, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in collaboration with St. Joseph’s College (Autonomous), Tiruchirappalli, and funded by the Kerala State Biodiversity Board, is organizing an International Seminar on “Degrading Ecosystems: Challenges, Consequences, and Conservation Strategies” on 19th and 20th November 2025 at Marian Hall. The event aims to explore critical environmental issues through discussions on biodiversity conservation, sustainable development, and ecosystem restoration. Researchers, scholars, and students are invited to present papers and participate in this two-day seminar.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML7240_20251119050606.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251119051201..jpg) Vimala College Secures Second Position in Calicut University Intercollegiate Weightlifting Championship 2025-2026 Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, proudly clinched the Second Position in the Calicut University Intercollegiate Weightlifting Championship 2025–2026. Their remarkable team spirit and consistency continue to uphold Vimala’s sporting excellence.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346%20\(1\)_20251119051251..jpg) Silver Medal for Vimala College in Calicut University Intercollegiate Badminton Championship 2025-2026 Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, is delighted to announce that our student secured the Silver Medal in the Calicut University Intercollegiate Badminton Championship 2025–2026. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251118113055..jpg) Alumnae Interactive Session: An Expatriates Voice The PG Department of English and Research Centre, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, organized an Alumnae Interactive Session titled “An Expatriate’s Voice” on 18 November 2025 at Liseiux Hall. Through the theme “Diasporic Dialogues,” the event offered students a meaningful platform to engage with global perspectives, cultural transitions, and expatriate experiences. The programme enriched students’ understanding of identity, belonging, and cross-cultural narratives while strengthening alumnae-student academic bonding.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML7181_20251118113055.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346%20\(1\)_20251112090223..jpg) Bronze Medal at All India Inter-University Fencing Championship 2025-2026 Angela Thobias and Anna Mariya of Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, proudly represented the University of Calicut at the All India Inter-University Fencing Championship 2025–2026 held at Amritsar. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251112090300..jpg) Bronze Medal in All India Inter-University Sabre Women Fencing Championship 2025-2026 Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, proudly congratulates its fencing team for securing the Bronze Medal at the All India Inter-University Sabre Women Fencing Championship 2025–2026, held at Amritsar. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251111090433..jpg) MSW Students Secure Second Runner-Up at All Kerala Social Work Student Congress - OPSTA 2025 The Second Year MSW students of Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, brought pride to the institution by winning the Second Runner-Up position at the All Kerala Social Work Student Congress – OPSTA 2025. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251107111746..jpg) Talk on Cyber Crimes: Myths and Realities The Postgraduate Departments of Sociology and Statistics at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, jointly organized a talk on “Cyber Crimes: Myths and Realities” on November 7, 2025, at Marian Hall. K.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML6737_20251107111746.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251107014525..jpg) Hindi Association Inauguration 2025 The Department of Hindi, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, inaugurated the Hindi Association for the academic year 2025–2026 on November 7, 2025, at Lisieux Hall. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251104115621..jpg) Kerala Piravi Day Celebrations at Vimala College Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, joyfully celebrated Kerala Piravi Day on November 1, 2025, to mark the formation of the state of Kerala. [ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251104121559..jpg) Employability Skills Training by CGPT in Association with Naandi Foundation - Mahindra Pride Classroom The Career Guidance, Placement & Training (CGPT) Cell of Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in association with the Naandi Foundation – Mahindra Pride Classroom, conducted an Employability Skills Training Programme from October 27 to November 11, 2025, at Marian Hall and Lissieux Seminar Hall. The training, designed for all final-year UG and PG students, focused on enhancing communication, time management, interview readiness, and teamwork. Interactive sessions and group discussions helped students develop the essential skills required to excel in their future careers.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML5935_20251104121559.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251101100132..jpg) Workshop on Computerized Psychological Testing Using PEBL The Postgraduate Department of Psychology, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, organized a workshop on Computerized Test – PEBL (Psychology Experiment Building Language) on October 30, 2025, from 9:30 a.m. to 10:30 a.m. The session was conducted in the Multimedia Room of the college, under the guidance of the Head of the Department, Dr. Sinto P. Anto, and with the support of the Principal, Dr. Sr. Beena Jose. The resource persons for the workshop were Ms. Aparna Suresh and Ms. Deva Reghuchandran Nair, Assistant Professors in the Department of Psychology. The session provided students with hands-on exposure to computerized psychological testing using PEBL, an open-source platform widely used for cognitive and experimental assessments.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/WhatsApp%20Image%202025-11-01%20at%209_20251101100132.jpeg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251101113001..jpg) Seminar on Stress Management in Higher Education The Department of Sociology, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, organized a seminar on “Stress Management in Higher Education” on October 31, 2025, at S.J. Hall. The session was led by Dr. Lakshmi M, Head of Behavioural Science, Kerala Police Academy, who emphasized practical approaches to understanding and managing stress in academic settings. The event aimed to enhance emotional well-being and resilience among students and educators.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/WhatsApp%20Image%202025-11-01%20at%2011_20251101113001.jpeg)

Balachandran (MLA, Thrissur) as the guest of honour.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/VIML5754_20251027030556.jpg)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/large/news%20_%20540x346_20251009030154..jpg) Observation of World Suicide Prevention Day 2025 Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, observed World Suicide Prevention Day on September 10, 2025, at 12 PM in the basketball court. The observance aimed to raise awareness about mental health and suicide prevention through creative expressions including a flash mob, musical drama, emotional wall with sticky notes, and pamphlet distribution, encouraging students to foster empathy, connection, and hope.  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/news/WhatsApp%20Image%202025-10-09%20at%202_20251009030154.jpeg)

#  College Initiatives 
![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/images/under-line_1.png)
[ ![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/middlemenu/medium/valbum_20210322103205..jpg) V-ALBUM  ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/photo-gallery)

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Administration
  

Women's education is a crucial factor that contributes to nation building and for the past fifty seven years we have been educating young women from different strata of our society. In order to facilitate the smooth functioning of the institution the college has an administartive body that functions in a diligent manner.
[Administrative Body](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/administration-body)

#### Agnipath Yojana
  

India’s youth is set to play a crucial role in realising the dream of building an AatmaNirbhar, Sashakt Bharat. And Agnipath scheme will take them a step closer. It is a unique opportunity to fulfil their dream of joining the Armed Forces & serving the Nation.The scheme will not only empower, discipline & skill youth with military ethos in civil society but also improve battle preparedness suited to the changing dynamics.
To know more about the scheme & how a career of honour can transform our Yuva Shakti into Agniveer click the link below .
[https://static.mygov.in/rest/s3fs-public/mygov_165537936251307401.pdf](https://static.mygov.in/rest/s3fs-public/mygov_165537936251307401.pdf)

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### At a Glance
  

Sprawling over 29.6 acres of land and situated in the cultural capital of a state lauded for its rich heritage, Vimala College, Thrissur was set up as a citadel of education, enlightenment and progress of young women in 1967. Undying enthusiasm, visionary diligence and a passion to bring out change, defined, moulded and transformed the dream of the CMC Management into what the College has become today. In her 58th year of inception, Vimala stands high with her flag unfurled to glory and true to the ideals of her founders whose vision is extolled in the words:
_"Our aim is the pursuit of intellectual and professional excellence for the total transformation of the human person for his/ her own enrichment and for the service of the society and the nation in the spirit of Jesus Christ"._
Bifurcated from St. Mary’s College and affiliated to the University of Calicut, Vimala offers 19 Undergraduate and 16 Post Graduate programmes along with other certificate and short term courses and is a Centre for Research in English, Physics, Commerce, Social work, Economics and Malayalam.  
  
Managed by Nirmala Province, Thrissur of the Congregation of Mother of Carmel (CMC), the College is under the religious jurisdiction of the Catholic Archbishop of Thrissur. With an enrolment of 2500 students, faculty strength of 147 members and an administrative team of 64 staff, the College is engaged in bringing into fruition the dream that shaped the legacy bequeathed to her by the founders. All efforts are made to sustain and enhance quality through the synchronisation of innovative measures and traditional values. With remarkable strides in curricular, co-curricular and extra- curricular spheres, the College has defined a concrete position for herself in the educational map of the state and envisions a journey of greater achievements in the future. The infrastructure and educational resources have been consistently expanded to meet the growing academic requirements. Technology enhanced teaching-learning experience and work culture have propelled the productivity forward and raised the merit of the institution.  
  
The College in high stead the vision and mission that forms the foundation of its very existence and accordingly engages in uncompromising and dedicated service to the field of education. Accredited at the national level with a Five Star status in 2001 by the NAAC, the institution has undergone three subsequent cycles of re-accreditation in 2009, 2014 and 2023 and presently holds the grade A+ with a CGPA of 3.45 on a 4 point scale. The University Grants Commission (UGC) conferred autonomy in 2015 and identified her as a College with Potential for Excellence in 2016. The Ministry of Human Resource Development, Government of India awarded the College the 52nd and 77th positions in the National Intuition Ranking Framework (NIRF) of the years 2017 and 2018 respectively. The college was in 100-150 band of NIRF Rankings in both 2019 and 2021 and got 99th position in NIRF 2020 Rankings.Our College has proudly attained the 80th position in the NIRF ranking of the academic year 2023 - 24 in College Category. Vimala College is a mentor college in NAAC Paramarsh Scheme from 2018. Vimala College has a full fledged DST -FIST funded laboratory and presently 6 science departments of college is supported under the DBT-STAR College Scheme. The college is also supported under RUSA (RASHTRIYA UCHCHATAR SHIKSHA ABHIYAN) by the Ministry of Human Resource Development, Government of India.The College is an ISO certified (ISO 9001:2015) institution and is awarded with 3.5 Stars for Innovation from IIC,MHRD, New Delhi.
In 2018 UGC approved two B.Voc programmes - Web Technology and Food processing, and community college offering three courses Diploma in Interior Architecture and Design (DIAD), Diploma in Digital Video Production and Diploma in Tourism Management and Hospitality. In 2022 the college is funded by DST (WISE - KIRAN Division) Govt. of India under Consolidation of University Research for Innovation and Excellence in Women Universities (CURIE) programme. 
Vimala College has secured the 6th Rank in Kerala in the Kerala Institutional Ranking Framework (KIRF) 2024.
Vimala College has secured the 78th Rank in India in the National Institutional Ranking Framework (NIRF) 2025.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### B.Voc
  

**I. B.Voc (Web Technology) (self financing 2021 onwards)**
**![](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/image/under-line_3.png)**
The accessibility to the whole world is available on your finger tips now, and this is considered as the biggest technical revolution so far happened. The size of the web increases exponentially every year and websites have become an integral part in today’s e-world. This course focuses on bridging the Gap in building efficient websites with perfect graphics and easy usability. Use of Cross platform tools, auto responsive websites, and search engine optimization skills are also needs of the hour which are also addressed in the course. This course provides skills on creation, design and maintenance of websites which is always a booming and sustainable area of employment.  
Job Roles:  
Diploma Level – Web Designer
Advance Diploma Level – Web Programmer & Web application Developer  
B.Voc Degree - Web Application Architect & Testers  
Our Industry Partners: InfoSphere Web Technologies, Palakkad; QuadLeo Tecchno  
Solutions, Kozhikkode: Linways Ekm
[**More...**](https://vimalacollege.edu.in/departments/BVoc)
* * *
  
**II. B.Voc (Food processing) (self financing 2021 onwards)**
**![](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/image/under-line_3.png)**
This course will provide an understanding about the meaning of processed foods and the role nutrition in the development of a new food product and types of preservatives and their role. Being informed and knowledgeable about how to produce new products is just as crucial as is creating products that have market value. This course provides a foundation for developing those new products better, and minimizing all types of risks along the way from product quality risks to health and business risks. Qualification in home science and  
specializations in food technology, nutrition or food services management could help with better opportunities. Similarly, certification in dietetic applied nutrition, food science and preservation helps candidates advance in career  
Job Roles:  
Diploma Level – Baking Technician  
Advance Diploma Level – Milk Product Analyst  
B.Voc Degree – Quality Controller/ Product Analyst  
Our Industry Partners: CAICO The Canning Industries Cochin Ltd, Thrissur; Manjilas Food  
Tech Pvt Ltd
[**More...**](https://vimalacollege.edu.in/departments/BVocFoodProcessing)
![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/images/foo-icon1.png)

**Departments Initiated Courses (Add-on)**  
**No** | **Department** | **Course Title**  
1 | English |  1. Certificate Course in French (Alliance Francaise) 2. Certificate Course in German (Goethe Zentrum) 3. Certificate Course in Acting Skills  
2 | Malayalam |  1. Malayalam Computing 2. Healthy Living 3. Certificate Course in Dubbing 4. Certificate Course in Media and Visual Journalism  
3 | Physical Education |  1. Fitness Self-Defence and Discipline through Martial Arts Training  
4 | Commerce |  1. Skill Enthancement Training Program  
5 |  Economics |  1. Sustainable Farming: Methods and Practices  
6 | Sociology |  1. Entrepreneurial Skill Development and Self Employment for Women 2. Course on Entry in Services through Competitive Exam 3. Add on Course on Participatory Rural Appraisal 4. Preparatory Course on Eligibiligy Test andCompetitive Examinations  
7 | Home Science |  1. Baking and Icing Craft  
8 | Statistics |  1. Certificate Course on Technical Writing with LaTeX 2. Certificate Course on Advanced TheoreticalResearch Using Wolfram Mathematics  
9 | Physics |  1.Observational Astronomy 2. Energy and Physics @ Home 3. Basic Course in Analytical Tools and Equipment  
10 | Mathematics |  1. Data Analytics Using Python  
11 | Computer Science |  1. Full Full Stack Development (MERN Stack) 2. Data Science and Analytics  
12 | Psychology |  1. Health Psychology 2. Learning Disability Management  
13 | Chemistry |  1. A Practical Framework to Research and Development 2. Sustainable Living - Theory and Practice 3. Scientific Waste Management  
14 | Botany |  1. Herbal Bearuty Practices 2. Herbarium Techniques and Management  
15 | Zoology |  1. Apiculture 2. Ornamental Fish Farming  
16 | Library |  1. Communicative English 2. Information Resources and Advanced DigitalLearning

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Community Enrichment Services
  

**Vimala Community Extension Centre (VCEC)**
**![](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/image/under-line_3.png)**
1. **Jeevani**
  * Implemented by the Department of Collegiate Education, Government of Kerala
  * Focuses on the mental well-being of the students in the government colleges in Kerala


Counselllers - Diya K M (2024-2025)
Malavika K S (2023-2024)
2. **Vimala Community Aid Sponsorship Scheme (VCASS)**
• Sponsored charity scheme  
• Sponsors : Staff, students and wellwishers  
• Beneficiary : 100 academically bright but socially and economically backward school children from the neighbouring schools
[Activities 2016 - 2021](https://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/VCASS\(1\).pdf)
[Activities 2021 - 2024](https://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/REPORT_2021-24.pdf)
3. **Vimala College Educational and Charitable Trust**
• Source of Corpus fund generation : staff, students, benefactors, well wishers and alumnae  
• Purpose : To provide financial assistance for the educational needs of the children of the ministerial staff of Vimala College and also that of the needy students of the college  
• The college also provides incentives, books, noon meal and so on for the economically deserving students  
• The assistance from the College Students’Aid Fund is also available to those students whose family income is very low
4. **Service Providing Centre**
  * Sponsored by the Ministry of Women and Child Development, Govt. of Kerala 
  * Provides services to women under the Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act 2005 


5. **Family Counselling Centre (FCC)**
  * Provides counselling service through trained counselors to students
  * Service extended to family court, colleges and schools in the locality


[Activities](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/FCC.pdf)
6 **CHILDLINE**
• Aim: To protect the rights of children and to ensure access to quality services to the poorest of poor.  
• Partnership with the Ministry of Woman and Child Development, Govt. of India and the UNICEF  
• Vimala College : A Nodal organization of CHILDLINE, Thrissur  
Contact No. 1098
[Activit](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/annual%20report%202020-%202021%20\(1\).pdf)ies

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Consultancy
  

The institution provides consultancy on a sceintific basis for the benefit of the entire community.The faculty members are encouraged to take up both remuneration based and free consultancy services.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Crest of Vimala
  

#  **THE CREST OF VIMALA**
![](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/LOGO%20NEW%20copy.jpg)
**T** he triangle represents God the Trinity, the community of love of the Father, the Son and the Holy Spirit.  
**T** he flame held up is the flame of truth and love to be lit up in the hearts of Vimalites.  
**T** he monogram ‘AM’ means AVE MARIA, the salutation of the archangel Gabriel at the dawn of redemption. It represents the Virgin Mother of God who watchfully presides over the kindling of the light of truth and love in the heart of each one of her beloved daughters through her constant intercession.
![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/images/foo-icon1.png)

#### CURIE PG 2022
  

The Department of Science and Technology (DST) implemented ‘Knowledge Involvement in Research Advancement through Nurturing (KIRAN)’ Scheme to provide various career opportunities for women scientists and technologists. It is primarily aimed to bring gender parity in the Science & Technology sector by inducting more women talent in the research & development domain through various programmes. CURIE & CURIE-AI facility of the Department under the KIRAN scheme, including other schemes, empowers women in science and technology.
In the year 2008-09, DST took a special initiative, “Consolidation of University Research for Innovation and Excellence (CURIE) in Women Universities, to support women universities for improving R&D infrastructure and enhance research facilities. The main objective of the CURIE programme is to train and create world class women scientists in all areas of Basic Sciences & Technology who would be able to carry out cutting-edge research in their respective fields in due course. In the year 2019, DST established an Artificial Intelligence lab in 6 CURIE beneficiary universities with the goal of fostering AI innovations and set up AI-friendly infrastructure to prepare skilled manpower for AI-based jobs in the future. This facility is exposing women students from these universities to different AI tools and will improve the employability of women in this upcoming sector.
Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur was sanctioned with the ‘CURIE’ Core Grant for women PG colleges in 2022 (DST/CURIE-PG/2022/33 (G) dated 30.03.2022.
**Organogram**
![](https://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/dfhzg.jpg)
**Objectives**
Optimal infrastructure facilities for post-graduate teaching and research
  * Renovation of existing laboratory space (no fresh constructions).
  * Modernization of laboratories involved in teaching and research.
  * Acquisition of essential equipment.
  * Up-gradation of existing facilities.
  * Networking and computational facilities including software and databases.
  * Scientific books (no journals).
  * Maintenance and refurbishing of existing and new facilities


**Target & Time-line**
**MONTH** |  **PLAN**  
---|---  
1-6 | 
  * Equipment Purchase & Installation
  * Appointing of Project Assistant
  * Infrastructure expansion

  
7-12 | 
  * Workshop for Equipment Familiarization for PG & Research students of the college

  
13-24 | 
  * Workshop on Research Methodology Interdisciplinary Webinars /Seminars /Workshops for PG & Research students of the college
  * Collaborations with institutes for research / interdisciplinary idea exchange
  * Consultancy Services extended to nearby institutions / research students

  
25-30 | 
  * Workshop on Plagiarism / Publishing Articles Promotion of PG Research projects
  * Improve intake of students via consultancy projects or research

  
30-36 | 
  * Publishing Research works

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Core Values
  

  1. **Focus on Stakeholder requisite**
  2. **Pursuit of Excellence**
  3. **Outcome and Value based education for transformational growth**
  4. **Inclusiveness and Respect for diversity**
  5. **Quest for Innovation**
  6. **Collegiality and Professionalism**
  7. **Institutional Wellness**
  8. **Environment Sustenance**
  9. **Participatory Governance**
  10. **Trust and Faith in God**

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### DBT-Star College Scheme
  

**About**
The Department of Biotechnology (DBT) under the Ministry of Science and Technology, Govt. of India, over the past 30 years, has given a new impetus to the developments in modern biology and biotechnology. The DBT is supporting undergraduate science education in the country under “Star College Scheme” and there has been considerable change in the scenario of basic science education. The Star College Scheme was initiated by DBT in 2008 to support colleges and universities offering undergraduate education to improve science teaching across the country.
Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur is sanctioned DBT STAR College Scheme in 2019 to Six Science Departments, Botany, Zoology, Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics and Statistics. Six departments conducted around 40 webinars in national and international levels for the UG students in 2020-21 and two FDP to strengthen our faculty. Also E tendered the Laboratory instruments, chemical and glasswares to the sanctioned departments.
The college envisions the Star College Scheme initiated by the DBT, Govt of India, as a tremendous opportunity for providing quality education in Science that would profoundly alter the structure of scientific practice itself. The very process of compiling this application helped us chisel to finesse the institutional objectives for Science education, which we have categorized into three heads: Firstly, education for women has become a priority in the world over considering various aspects like empowerment, enlightenment and progress. Yet, the visible presence of women of eminence in science is still sparse and demands focused attention, motivation and promotion. Vimala being a College for women, with 8 Science departments offering 10 programmes that have consistently recorded outstanding academic performance, the promotion of various aspects relating to Science is sure to bolster and carve brilliant minds.
* * *
**Organogram**
![](https://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/Untitled-1\(2\).jpg)
* * *
**Enhancement of infrastructure**
  * ·Expansion of laboratory facilities (Multiple units of instruments, procuring new equipment and upgrading of existing facilities to address emerging trends in discipline)
  * ·Enhancement of library resources


**Advancement of quality in teaching, learning process**
  * ·Integration of technology (preparation of e-content for CEC – Under Graduate , SWAYAM , NPTEL or any other Government initiative for institutional Learning Management System)
  * ·Visit to premium institutions and laboratories
  * ·Interface with eminent scientists and industrialists
  * ·Professional development programmes for faculty
  * ·Training for laboratory staff


**Inculcation of research culture**
  * ·Research projects in collaboration with organizations or institutions of eminence
  * ·Augmentation of research output


**Curriculum modification Skill acquisition programmes**
  * ·Value addition (courses, lectures, seminars, workshops, etc)
  * ·Experiential learning
  * ·Internship


**Incubation of idea to product**
  * ·Motivate students to original thinking and express innovative ideas
  * ·Self employment, start up focusing on the indigenous natural resources of the State


**Outreach and Extension**
  * ·Extend Science oriented service to the villages adopted under the UBA scheme

##  Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur 
###  NAAC Reaccredited (4th cycle): A+ Grade - CGPA 3.45 
###  College with Potential for Excellence (2016-2020) 
###  Affiliated to the University of Calicut 
###  Mentor College in NAAC Paramarsh Scheme 
###  Supported Under RUSA & DBT Star College Scheme 
###  ISO 9001 - 2015 Certified 
![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads/departments/banner/1627011286_2021-04-15_11-36-39.jpg)
#  Department of English 
#  Department Overview 
![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/images/under-line_3.png)
The Department of English, that offers B. A. English Language and Literature, B. A. Functional English, M. A. English Language and Literature and PhD in English was established in 1946 at St. Mary’s College, Thrissur. The Department shifted to Vimala College, Thrissur following the bifurcation of the Colleges in 1967. The Department that started functioning in 1967 at Vimala College with Sr. Mary Leo as the Head, today, stands on a foundation of rich literary, academic artistic and research endeavours, that has been moulded by the meritorious service of 24 faculty members who have completed service till date. The Heads of Departments along with the Professors have contributed much to the introduction, promotion and propagation of liberal and updated education in the College. Among the milestones of the Department are, recognition of the Department as a Research Centre of the University of Calicut in 1978; the introduction and organisation of the annual Poets’ Artiste’s Day Competition in memory of Sr. Leo, an inter-departmental contest that awards excellence in theatre and performance; regularisation of the B. A. Functional English programme in 2010; the Best Literary Artist Competition in memory of Dr. Sr. Cleopatra, an annual competition that began as an inter-departmental contest but has been expanded into an inter-collegiate event since 2018; renovation of the Language Lab into a multi-media facility - Chomsky Convergence Centre in 2018 and introduction of one year courses like Business English Certificate of the Cambridge Language Assessment Programme, Trinity GESE Exams of Trinity College, London and B. Voc programmes. The Department, a team of 17 faculty members, is presently led by the HoD, Dr Sr. Tency Varghese P.
  * [ Add-On Courses ](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/departments/english/addoncourses)

##  Vision 
To Bring Holistic Transformation in Students by Implementing Innovative Teaching and Learning Skills. 
##  Mission 
To Create and Disseminate Knowledge and Language through Research and Creative Enquiry, Teaching and Learning.

#### Mentor college-NAAC PARAMARSH Scheme
  

College has been recognized under**PARAMARSH - “UGC Scheme for Mentoring NAAC Accreditation Aspirant”** to enable Higher Education Institutions to upgrade their academic performance, enhance their overall quality and get themselves accredited by NAAC
#### **Mentee Colleges  
![](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/image/under-line_3.png)**
1. Don Bosco College, Ollukara, Mannuthy, Thrissur, Kerala 680651
2. Yuvakshetra Institute of Management Studies, Ezhakkad, Yuvakhetra Rd, Mundur-II, Palakkad, Kerala 678631
3. Sahrdaya College of Advanced Studies (Unaided), Kodakara – 680684
4. Govt Arts & Science College Ollur, Thrissur, Kerala 680306
5. Chetana College of Media and Performing Arts (Unaided), Thiruthoor Temple Rd, Nehru Nagar, Chiyyaram, Thrissur, Kerala 680006

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

##  Introduction & Orientation on Career Counsellor Training

The PG Department of Psychology, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in collaboration with JIB Career Academy, Thrissur, is organizing a seminar on Introduction & Orientation on Career Counsellor Training. The session will be led by Ms. Raysa P S, Chief Instructor, and aims to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of career counselling as a profession, its scope, training pathways, and opportunities in the field of guidance and counselling. 
  * [ ](http://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=www.vimalacollege.edu.in/events/Introduction-&-Orientation-on-Career-Counsellor-Training&t=Introduction%20&%20Orientation%20on%20Career%20Counsellor%20Training)

##  Sr. Canisia General Quiz Competition 2026

The Post Graduate & Research Department of Chemistry, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, is organizing the Sr. Canisia General Quiz Competition 2026 to promote knowledge, critical thinking, and intellectual curiosity among students. The competition offers an engaging platform for participants to test their general awareness, analytical skills, and teamwork in a spirited academic environment.

##  Rachana: One-Day Short Story Workshop & Research Seminar

Join us at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, for a comprehensive workshop exploring the nuances of Malayalam fiction. Organized by the Malayalam Research Department in collaboration with the UGC Center for Women’s Studies, this event features sessions by eminent scholars V. Vijaykumar & Dr. Rajendran Edathumkara and writers including Ashtamoorthi & Jinsha Ganga. Dive into the world of creative writing, women’s narratives, and literary research. 
  * [ ](http://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=www.vimalacollege.edu.in/events/Rachana--One-Day-Short-Story-Workshop-&-Research-Seminar&t=Introduction%20&%20Orientation%20on%20Career%20Counsellor%20Training)

##  Participatory Rural Appraisal (PRA) Field Programme at Kadangode Gram Panchayat

The PG & Research Department of Sociology, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, is organizing a three-day Participatory Rural Appraisal (PRA) programme at Kadangode Gram Panchayat from 21st to 23rd January 2026. The programme aims to provide students with hands-on experience in community-based research, enabling them to understand rural social realities, local governance, and development practices through active interaction with the community and participatory methods. 
  * [ ](http://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=www.vimalacollege.edu.in/events/Participatory-Rural-Appraisal-PRA-Field-Programme-at-Kadangode-Gram-Panchayat&t=Introduction%20&%20Orientation%20on%20Career%20Counsellor%20Training)

##  Mock Recruitment Workshop

The Career Guidance, Placement & Training Cell of Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in association with DePaul Institute of Science & Technology, Angamaly, is organizing a Mock Recruitment Workshop to provide students with real-world recruitment experience. The workshop will include simulated group discussions, expert panel interviews, and performance analytics, helping students build confidence, improve soft skills, and prepare effectively for actual placement drives.

##  Honouring Our Distinguished Alumna - Ms. Shameera Shabeer

The PG & Research Department of Sociology, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, is proud to honour its distinguished alumna, Ms. Shameera Shabeer, Chairperson of the Health–Education Standing Committee, Engandiyoor Grama Panchayat. This recognition celebrates her remarkable contribution to public service and community development, and aims to inspire students through her achievements and leadership in the field of social welfare and governance.

##  Magnus 2k26: National Level Management Fest

Hosted by the PG Department of Commerce and Research at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, Magnus 2k26 celebrates 50 years of excellence. This flagship event features a dynamic "Business Track" with both management and non-management competitions designed to showcase student leadership, creativity, and strategic thinking. Date: 16th January 2026 Venue: Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur Registration Deadline: 15th January 2026, 11:59 PM (Online) Highlight Events: Best Management Team, HR Game, Marketing Game, Business Quiz, Group Dance, and more

##  Self Defence: A Life Skill for Every Young Woman

The Department of Psychology, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in collaboration with Jeevani – Centre for Students’ Well Being, is organizing a self-defence awareness and training session titled “Self Defence: A Life Skill for Every Young Woman.” The session will be led by Mr. Joy Varghese K, Technical Secretary, Kerala Judo Association, and aims to empower young women with essential self-protection skills, confidence, and awareness for personal safety and well-being.

##  Python for Data Analysis: A Two-Day Hands-on Training Program

Join the Post Graduate & Research Department of Statistics at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in collaboration with the UGC Centre for Women's Studies, for an intensive, two-day workshop designed to master the essentials of data science using Python. Led by AI and ML mentor Namitha R, this program offers practical, hands-on experience in the Statistics Computer Lab. Participants will dive into the Python ecosystem, covering everything from Jupyter notebooks and basic syntax to advanced data manipulation with Pandas. The curriculum includes comprehensive training in Data Visualization using industry-standard libraries like Matplotlib, Seaborn, and Plotly, as well as techniques for exploratory data analysis and descriptive statistics. Whether you are a student, research scholar, or faculty member, this workshop provides the technical foundation needed to thrive in the data-driven landscape of 2026. Date: January 15th & 16th, 2026 Time: 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM Venue: Statistics Computer Lab 2, Vimala College, Thrissur

##  Empowering Research Excellence: High-Impact Publishing with Elsevier

Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in association with Elsevier, proudly presents a Two-Day National Workshop titled “Empowering Faculty and Research Scholars for High-Impact Research Publishing – Author Heroes: Elsevier.” Organized by the IQAC, R&D Cell, and Faculty Enrichment Committee, the workshop aims to equip faculty members and research scholars with essential skills in scholarly writing, ethical publishing, journal selection, peer-review navigation, and the responsible use of generative AI in research. Led by Scientific Editors from Elsevier, the sessions will offer expert insights, practical strategies, and hands-on guidance to enhance research visibility, quality, and global impact. The workshop will be held on 8th & 9th January 2026 at Marian Hall, providing an excellent platform for academic growth and research empowerment.

##  World Antimicrobial Resistance Awareness Week

The Department of Zoology, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in association with the AMR Hub & Spoke Lab, General Hospital Thrissur, is organizing a special event for World Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) Awareness Week. ##  International Seminar on Degrading Ecosystems: Challenges, Consequences and Conservation Strategies

The Department of Botany, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, is organizing an International Seminar on “Degrading Ecosystems: Challenges, Consequences, and Conservation Strategies” on November 19–20, 2025. Funded by the Kerala State Biodiversity Board and held in collaboration with St. Joseph’s College (Autonomous), Tiruchirappalli, the seminar aims to foster academic dialogue and research on biodiversity conservation, sustainable development, and ecosystem management. ##  Vismayam2025

We invite schools and students to explore the diverse and engaging exhibitions of knowledge across various disciplines: 1. Botany - Mustard Unlock the World of the Plant Kingdom and explore the wonders of nature. 2. Chemistry - Rasatantra 2K25 Dive into the magic of chemistry and discover the reactions that shape our world. 3. Computer Science - Eight Bits 2K25 Unlock the power of technology and see the innovations that drive the future. 4. Zoology - Fauna Focus 2K25 Where science meets the wild; learn about the fascinating animal kingdom. 5. Psychology - Psychexpo 2K25 Unveil the mysteries of the mind and understand human behavior. 6. Physics - Phy-Esta 2K25 A journey through the wonders of physics and the forces that govern our universe. 7. Sociology - Sociovision Explore the science of social life and the dynamics of human society. 8. Mathematics - Polaris 2025 Unlock the patterns and possibilities within the world of numbers and equations. 9. Home Science Your gateway to discover, design, and learn fashion, food, and hospitality. Home Science for a Lifetime of Excellence. 10. Physical Education - Prayatna Effort is the driving force behind hard work and determination in sports. We invite all schools and students to register and participate in this exciting fest. Register by visiting https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSddDkZ1X3GYyMSPKwe3L_ZqUgrSqACMqchichjfe-ssm8OPtQ/viewform?pli=1 For more details or inquiries, contact 9846678530 | 9495885202

##  National Nutrition Month Celebration 2025 - Eat Right for a Better Life

The Department of Home Science, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, proudly celebrates National Nutrition Month 2025 from 17th to 22nd September under the theme “Eat Right for a Better Life.” The week-long celebration includes an inauguration and invited talk on reviving millets, interactive nutrition classes for different age groups, a flameless cooking competition (Nutri-Delights), an online quiz, and the Nutrigirl preliminary round. The events aim to promote awareness about balanced diets, healthy living, and the importance of nutrition across all stages of life.

##  Mehfil-e-Qawwali: A Celebration of India's Linguistic & Musical Heritage

The Department of English at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in association with the UGC Centre for Women's Studies, Saarang Music Club, Department of Hindi, and the English Alumnae Association, is organizing a "Mehfil-e-Qawalli." This event, with the theme "The Lyric Loom," is a celebration of India's linguistic and musical heritage. The event will be held on August 22, 2025, at 10:30 am in the Auditorium. This event is more than a performance; it is a dialogue where words and music unite to tell timeless stories. Through the poetry of qawwali, which is woven in languages like Hindi, Urdu, Punjabi, and Persian, the event will journey through themes of love, devotion, and spiritual ecstasy. The setting will recreate a traditional "mehfil" atmosphere, with renowned vocalists, tabalists, and musicians at the heart of the auditorium, surrounded by the audience in a circle of shared experience. The music carries the centuries-old tradition of Sufi thought and the living legacy of India's diverse voices 
  * [ ](http://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=www.vimalacollege.edu.in/events/Mehfil-e-Qawwali-A-Celebration-of-Indias-Linguistic-&-Musical-Heritage&t=Introduction%20&%20Orientation%20on%20Career%20Counsellor%20Training)
  * [ ](http://twitter.com/home?status=www.vimalacollege.edu.in/events/Mehfil-e-Qawwali-A-Celebration-of-Indias-Linguistic-&-Musical-Heritage)
  * [ ](whatsapp://send?text=www.vimalacollege.edu.in/events/Mehfil-e-Qawwali-A-Celebration-of-Indias-Linguistic-&-Musical-Heritage)

##  Two-Day National Conference on Revitalizing Kerala Economy: Issues & Challenges

The PG & Research Department of Economics, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, is organizing a Two-Day National Conference on “Revitalizing Kerala Economy: Issues & Challenges” on 21st and 22nd August 2025 at Marian Hall, Vimala College. The conference aims to explore innovative strategies for Kerala’s economic revitalization with a focus on sustainability, inclusive growth, and resilience. Key themes include fiscal management, industrial development, agriculture, tourism, and welfare measures. The event will bring together academicians, research scholars, industry experts, and students to present papers, exchange insights, and propose solutions for addressing Kerala’s economic challenges. Sponsored by South Indian Bank Ltd, this conference will provide a vital platform for critical discussions, research, and policy-oriented dialogue to foster a stronger and more sustainable economy for Kerala.

##  Invited Talk on Significance of Plant Taxonomy and Identification Methods in the AI Era

The Department of Botany, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, is hosting an invited talk on Significance of Plant Taxonomy and Identification Methods in the AI Era on 14th August 2025 from 9:00 AM to 10:30 AM. The session will be led by Dr. Suresh V, Associate Professor, PG & Research Department of Botany, Government Victoria College, Palakkad. This event will explore the evolving role of artificial intelligence in enhancing traditional taxonomy methods, improving plant identification accuracy, and advancing botanical research.
