##  Independence Day Speech Competition

The Department of Political Science in collaboration with the PG Department of Psychology at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, is organizing a Speech Competition as part of the Independence Day celebrations. The competition will be held on August 14th from 8:30 AM to 10:30 AM at the Silver Jubilee Seminar Hall. The topic for the competition is "സൗഹൃദം സ്വാതന്ത്ര്യത്തിലേക്കുള്ള പാത" (Saurhridham Swatantryathilekkulla Patha - Friendship is the path to freedom). The duration of the speech is 5 minutes. Attractive cash prizes will be awarded to the first, second, and third-place winners.

##  RITI 10.0 - Intercollegiate Tech Fest

The Department of Computer Science, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, is all set to host RITI 10.0, the landmark 10th edition of its prestigious annual intercollegiate techno fest, on 13 August 2025. This vibrant celebration of technology and innovation will bring together talented minds from across colleges to compete, collaborate, and create. The fest will feature a wide array of thrilling events, including coding challenges, UI/UX design contests, technical quizzes, treasure hunts, and AI & tech exhibitions, giving participants the perfect platform to showcase their skills and ingenuity. In addition to competitive events, RITI 10.0 will also host engaging workshops, interactive sessions, and networking opportunities with tech enthusiasts and experts. With a blend of creativity, problem-solving, and cutting-edge technology, RITI 10.0 promises to be an unforgettable experience for participants and spectators alike. Mark your calendars and be part of this journey where ideas turn into innovations, and innovations inspire the future.

##  Manahsasatram 2025: Grand Psychology Fest Celebrates Minds in Motion

The Post Graduate Department of Psychology, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, hosted Manahsasatram 2025 — a vibrant three-day National Psychology Fest from August 11 to 13, 2025. Themed "Psychology in Action: Building Mentally Healthy Communities," the event featured a National Seminar, research paper presentations, and dynamic competitions like Alter Ego (theme fashion show), Thanatos (dance), Psyquest (quiz), Psych Ink (poster making), and Clandestine (psych-based games). Eminent psychologists and academicians from across India enriched the event with insightful sessions on community mental health and resilience. The fest successfully merged academic depth with creative expression, reinforcing the role of psychology in shaping inclusive, mentally resilient communities.

##  AMEDEO 2025 - Intercollegiate Chemistry Fest

The PG Department of Chemistry, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, proudly presents AMEDEO 2025, an intercollegiate Chemistry Fest on 4th August 2025. The fest features a vibrant lineup of intellectually stimulating and creatively charged events including Ionix (Quiz), Recall-RX (Memory Game), Z=118 (Periodic Table Arrangement), Chem Canvas (Live Poster Making), Vibeon (Group Dance), and Lustrous Line (Fashion Show). Designed to ignite scientific spirit and artistic flair, AMEDEO 2025 welcomes participants from colleges across the region for a day of competition, creativity, and collaboration.

##  Report Release: The Uncelebrated Wanderers

On 28th July 2025, the Department of Zoology in association with Aranyakam Nature Foundation released the report The Uncelebrated Wanderers at Lissieux Hall. The report offered insights from citizen science on the distribution and ecology of Kerala’s golden jackal (Canis aureus), emphasizing the species’ importance in local biodiversity.

##  Hands-On Training on Working and Maintenance of Laboratory Instruments

Chemistry Lab and Instrumentation RoThe PG Department of Chemistry, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, is organizing a Hands-On Training Programme on the Working and Maintenance of Laboratory Instruments on July 28–29, 2025, from 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM. The training will be held at the Chemistry Lab and Instrumentation Room, aiming to familiarize participants with laboratory equipment such as pH meters, colorimeters, UV spectrometers, and more. ##  Toppers Day Celebration by PG & Research Department of Malayalam

The PG & Research Department of Malayalam at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, celebrated Toppers Day on 25th July 2025 at Marian Hall. The event honoured academic excellence by felicitating top-performing students from UG and PG batches. The celebration was a proud moment of recognition, inspiring peers to strive for academic distinction. The department appreciated the hard work and commitment of the students who excelled across all semesters. ##  Care Nest: A Touch of Compassion by the Department of English

As part of its Social Outreach and Inclusive Education initiative, the PG Department of English and Research Centre, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, organized Care Nest on 19th July 2025 at AMHA, Thrissur. Students engaged with mentally challenged adults and children with autism to explore the power of language, compassion, and connection—fostering empathy and inclusion beyond the classroom.

##  Career Talk on The Future is Yours: Career Opportunities Beyond the Lab

The Post Graduate Department of Chemistry, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, organized a career guidance session titled "The Future is Yours: Career Opportunities Beyond the Lab" on 16th July 2025 at 2:00 PM in SJ Hall. The session featured Dr. Eldho Elias, Scientific Officer, Regional Forensic Science Laboratory, Thrissur. The interactive session focused on diverse career paths available to Chemistry graduates outside traditional lab roles, inspiring students to explore emerging fields such as forensic science, quality control, and environmental science. The event was coordinated by Dr. Nimmy Kuriakose, under the leadership of Dr. Nancy Mathew (HOD) and Dr. Sr. Beena Jose (Principal).

##  Career Guidance Session: Careers Ahead - Begin with a Goal

The Department of Computer Science and the PG Department of Commerce & Research at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, jointly organized an engaging career guidance session titled "Careers Ahead: Begin with a Goal" on 7th July 2025 at Lisieux Seminar Hall. The session was specifically designed for first-year B.Sc. Computer Science, B.Com, and B.Voc Web Technology students. Mr. Akhilesh A, Soft Skill Trainer, led the session, which covered essential topics including career planning for freshers, job roles in the government sector, preparation strategies for competitive exams like SSC, UPSC, PSC, RRB, IBPS, and career opportunities in the private sector.

##  Alumni Talk: Economics Unfolded - Tools, Trajectories and New Frontiers

The PG & Research Department of Economics, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, organized an insightful alumni talk titled “Economics Unfolded: Tools, Trajectories, and New Frontiers” on 2nd July 2025 at 10:00 AM. The session featured Akhila P, Research Scholar at the Centre for Development Studies, Trivandrum, who shared her academic and research journey while highlighting emerging trends and tools in the field of Economics. The event served as a valuable platform for students to engage with alumni and explore advanced perspectives in economic studies.

The Department of Botany and the Biodiversity Club of Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, conducted an Intercollegiate Quiz Competition on 5th June 2025 at Lisieux Hall as part of the World Environment Day celebrations. The quiz, centered on Biodiversity, Animal and Plant Ecology, and Conservation, witnessed enthusiastic participation from various colleges, promoting environmental awareness and academic engagement. 
  * [ ](http://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=www.vimalacollege.edu.in/events/IntercollegiateQuizCompetition&t=Introduction%20&%20Orientation%20on%20Career%20Counsellor%20Training)

##  Invited Talk on Plastic Pollution and its Impacts - World Environment Day 2025

As part of World Environment Day 2025 celebrations, the Department of Botany of Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, organized an Invited Talk on Plastic Pollution and its Impacts on 5th June 2025 at Lisieux Hall. The session was led by Dr. Deepesh V, Scientist 'D', Central Pollution Control Board, Bangalore, who shed light on the environmental and health challenges posed by plastic pollution. 
  * [ ](http://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=www.vimalacollege.edu.in/events/InvitedTalkPlasticPollutionandImpacts&t=Introduction%20&%20Orientation%20on%20Career%20Counsellor%20Training)

##  Beautification Project Launch - Garbage-Free Kerala Initiative

Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, officially launched the “Beautification of College Road” project on 5th June 2025 at Marian Hall, under the 'Garbage-Free Kerala' initiative. The event was inaugurated by Hon. Mayor Sri M. K. Varghese and supported by NSS Units 120 & 43. The campaign includes eco-awareness boards, waste segregation bins, and native greenery installations for a cleaner, greener campus. 
  * [ ](http://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=www.vimalacollege.edu.in/events/BeautificationProjectLaunchGarbageFreeKeralaInitiative&t=Introduction%20&%20Orientation%20on%20Career%20Counsellor%20Training)

##  Tree Planting Ceremony - World Environment Day

As part of World Environment Day 2025, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, hosted a meaningful Tree Planting Ceremony to promote sustainability. The event featured the presence of Dr. Raghavendra Bhatta and key figures from ICAR, KAU, and KVK Thrissur. Organized by the Departments of Physics, Sociology, and NSS, the initiative aimed to instill environmental responsibility among students.

##  Environment Day Celebrations - NSS & Departments of Physics and Sociology

Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, celebrated World Environment Day on 5th June 2025 with a special campaign organized by NSS Units 43 & 120 in association with the Departments of Physics and Sociology. The programme was graced by eminent personalities including Dr. Raghavendra Bhatta (Deputy Director General, ICAR), Dr. Venkitasubramanian (Director, ATARI Bengaluru), and others, fostering awareness and action for environmental conservation.

##  Photography Competition by PG Department of English and Research Centre

The PG Department of English and Research Centre, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, is organizing a Photography Competition in connection with World Environment Day. Theme: Environment/Nature Students are invited to capture the beauty of nature through their lens. 📅 Last date to submit entries: 9th June 2025 📲 Submit via WhatsApp: 9605053333 🏆 The best click wins ₹500 and a certificate! Guidelines: Maximum 2 entries per class Full HD resolution No editing allowed Only landscape mode Let your lens tell nature’s story!

##  Guest Lecturer Interview Schedule - Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur

Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, invites eligible candidates for the State & Management Guest Lecturer Interviews for the academic year 2025-2026. The interviews will be held on 22 May 2025 (Malayalam, Sanskrit, Hindi, Commerce, Sociology) and 23 May 2025 (Social Work, Political Science, Home Science, Zoology, Computer Science, Mathematics) at 10:00 AM. Candidates must present their original certificates at the time of the interview and preference will be given to those who have qualified UGC NET or hold a PhD. All applicants must fill out the Google Form available under the "Register" link. For more details, check the Brochure. 
  * [ ](http://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=www.vimalacollege.edu.in/events/Guest-Lecturer-Interview-Schedule&t=Introduction%20&%20Orientation%20on%20Career%20Counsellor%20Training)

##  National Seminar on Hindi Literature and Indian Knowledge Systems

Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, and Little Flower College (Autonomous), Guruvayoor, are jointly organizing a National Seminar (online) on ‘भारतीय ज्ञान परंपरा में हिंदी साहित्य की भूमिका’ on 18th March 2025. The seminar will feature Dr. Sushila S Vyas, Associate Professor, Department of Hindi, SMT C R Gardi Arts College, Gujarat, as the resource person.

##  Practical Visit to Kerala Fire & Rescue Services Academy

The PG Department of Sociology, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in association with the Fire Force Academy, is organizing a Practical Visit to the Kerala Fire and Rescue Services Academy, Thrissur on March 3, 2025, at 10:30 AM. This hands-on learning experience will provide students with valuable insights into fire safety, emergency response, and disaster management. Faculty Coordinator: Dr. Binu K Student Coordinators: Adwitha Aravind & Bhadra S Menon Principal: Dr. Sr Beena Jose

##  Workshop on Family Therapy - Department of Social Work

The Department of Social Work, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, is organizing a Workshop on Family Therapy on 27th & 28th February 2025. The session will be led by Dr. Reni Thomas, Assistant Professor, Department of Psychiatry, St. John's Medical College, Bangalore. This workshop aims to provide insights into effective family therapy techniques and practices. 📅 Date: 27th & 28th February 2025 ⏰ Time: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM 📍 Venue: Room No. 510 & Silver Jubilee Seminar Hall

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Graduate Attributes
  

**Portrait of a Vimala Graduate  
![](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/image/under-line_3.png)**  

  1. Comprehensive subject knowledge
  2. Inquisitiveness and Unquenched yearning for learning
  3. Effective and Discerning utilization of information
  4. Self-Knowledge and Articulation of potential and talents
  5. Engaged, Committed and Accountable negotiator of humanitarian concerns
  6. Ethical competency, Decorum and Propriety
  7. Leadership qualities and Zeal for teamwork
  8. Readiness for Calling : Vocation, Mission, Career
  9. Proficiency in Communication and Use of technology
  10. Problem solving and Analytical skills
  11. Life skills and Emotional quotient to overcome unforeseen challenges
  12. Active crusader for environment sustenance
  13. Accountable and Responsible citizenry
  14. Inspired to be distinctive and unique in chosen sphere
  15. Deep rooted faith in the Divine

#### History
  

#### Internal Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC)  
![](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/image/under-line_3.png)
The Internal Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) of the College was set up as part of the post-accreditation Quality Sustenance Activities recommended by the National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC). The Vimala College IQAC was constituted in the year 2003 under the chairmanship of Principal with faculty representatives from each department and stakeholders. The prime task of the IQAC is to develop a system for conscious, consistent and catalytic improvement in the overall performance of Vimala College. The Cell is actively involved in planning and implementing quality initiatives in the campus towards promoting academic excellence.
![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/images/foo-icon1.png)

#### Mission

#### Institutional Distinctiveness
  

  1. **Academic Excellence**
  2. **Holistic, Value based education**
  3. **Outstanding performance in Sports and Arts**
  4. **Commitment to Community**
  5. **Passion and Perseverance for Environmental Sustenance**

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Vimala International Aquatic Academy Thrissur
  

Vimala International Aquatic Academy was inaugurated in the year 2016 and hosted several national and state level swimming competitions.
**Objectives**
  * Trains life skills around water 
  * Teaches how to survive water incase of accidents
  * Train how to maintain proper health
  * Provides coaching for various competitions


_**Daily Hours of Operation**_
**6 am - 10 am**
**3 pm - 7 pm**
**(Second Saturday & Sunday Holiday)**  

  
_**MEMBERSHIP FEES**_(without trainer)
**Term** | **Category** | **Amount**  
---|---|---  
Daily Pass | Per Person | 1hr Rs.180  
2hr Rs.240  
Monthly | Individual | 3540/-  
Family | 7080/-  
Yearly | Individual | 17700/-  
Family | 35400/-  
5 Year | Individual | 29500/-  
Family | 53100/-  
10 Year | Individual | 41300/-  
Family | 64900/-  
15 Year | Individual | 47200/-  
Couple | 70800/-  
Family | 118000/-  
One Generation | Diamond Card | 177000/-  
Two Generation | Platinum Card | 265500/-  
_**TRAINING FEES**_
  * _**​**_**KIDS BELOW THE AGE OF 6 YEARS ARE NOT PERMITTED **
  * **GIRLS, BOYS & LADIES – 2950/-**
  * **MEN (15 YEARS ABOVE ) – 3540/-**
  * **CONTACT NUMBER - 8304030646, 0487-2326046**

#### INTRODUCING VIMALA
  

Sprawling over 26 acres of land and situated in the cultural capital of a state lauded for its rich heritage, Vimala College, Thrissur was set up as a citadel of education, enlightenment and progress of young women in 1967. Undying enthusiasm, visionary diligence and a passion to bring out change, defined, moulded and transformed the dream of the CMC Management into what the College has become today. In her 58th year of inception, Vimala stands high with her flag unfurled to glory and true to the ideals of her founders whose vision is extolled in the words: Our aim is the pursuit of intellectual and professional excellence for the total transformation of the human person for his/ her own enrichment and for the service of the society and the nation in the spirit of Jesus Christ. Bifurcated from St. Mary’s College and affiliated to the University of Calicut, Vimala offers 19 Undergraduate and 16 Post Graduate programmes along with other certificate and short term courses and is a Centre for Research in English, Physics, Commerce, Social work, Economics and Malayalam.
Managed by Nirmala Province, Thrissur of the Congregation of Mother of Carmel (CMC), the College is under the religious jurisdiction of the Catholic Archbishop of Thrissur. With an enrolment of 2934 students, faculty strength of 147 members and an administrative team of 64 staff, the College is engaged in bringing into fruition the dream that shaped the legacy bequeathed to her by the founders. All efforts are made to sustain and enhance quality through the synchronisation of innovative measures and traditional values. With remarkable strides in curricular, co-curricular and extra- curricular spheres, the College has defined a concrete position for herself in the educational map of the state and envisions a journey of greater achievements in the future. The infrastructure and educational resources have been consistently expanded to meet the growing academic requirements. Technology enhanced teaching-learning experience and work culture have propelled the productivity forward and raised the merit of the institution.
The College in high stead the vision and mission that forms the foundation of its very existence and accordingly engages in uncompromising and dedicated service to the field of education. Accredited at the national level with a Five Star status in 2001 by the NAAC, the institution has undergone three subsequent cycles of re-accreditation in 2009, 2014 and 2023 and presently holds the grade A+ with a CGPA of 3.45 on a 4 point scale. The University Grants Commission (UGC) conferred autonomy in 2015 and identified her as a College with Potential for Excellence in 2016. The Ministry of Human Resource Development, Government of India awarded the College the 52nd and 77th positions in the National Intuition Ranking Framework (NIRF) of the years 2017 and 2018 respectively. The college was in 100-150 band of NIRF Rankings in both 2019 and 2021 years and got 99th position in NIRF 2020 Rankings, in 2024 College is ranked in 80th position. Vimala College is a mentor college in NAAC Paramarsh Scheme from 2018. Vimala College has a full fledged DST -FIST funded laboratory and presently 6 science departments of college is supported under DBT-STAR college Scheme. The College is an ISO certified (ISO 9001:2015) institution and is awarded with 3.5 Stars for Innovation from IIC,MHRD,New Delhi.
In 2018 UGC approved two B.Voc programmes - Web Technology and Food processing, and Community College offering three courses Diploma in Interior Architecture and Design (DIAD), Diploma in Digital Video Production and Diploma in Tourism Management and Hospitality.
Vimala College has secured the 6th Rank in Kerala in the Kerala Institutional Ranking Framework (KIRF) 2024.
Vimala College has secured the 78th Rank in India in the National Institutional Ranking Framework (NIRF) 2025.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Institutional Accreditations & Rankings
  

**1. NAAC Accreditations**
  * [Vimala College Reaccredited with A+ Grade, CGPA : 3.45 on a 4 point scale by NAAC 2023](https://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/4%20Cycle%20NAAC%20-%2014%20March%202023.pdf)
  * [Vimala College Reaccredited with A Grade, CGPA : 3.5 on a 4 point scale by NAAC 2014](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/NAAC%20-%2005%20May%202014.pdf) ​​
  * [Vimala College Reaccredited with A Grade, CGPA : 3.30 on a 4 point scale by NAAC 2009](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/NAAC%20-%2029%20January%202009.pdf) ​
  * [NAAC honours Vimala with Five Star accreditation 2001](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/NAAC%20-%2019%20January%202001.pdf) ​


​**2. Autonomous Status**
  * [Declaration of Autonomous Status 2016](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/Autonomy\(1\).pdf)​


**3. College conferred the status of College with Potential for Excellence in 2016**

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Internship Guidance Cell
  

The Internship Guidance Cell at Vimala College (Autonomous) is a dynamic body, envisioned to open up trajectories of diverse internship opportunities and get the student community in touch with the larger horizons of employability and practical experience in their disciplines.
_**Objectives**_
  * Identify Internship programmes suitable for each discipline
  * Disseminate information and guide the students to explore various internship opportunities
  * Offer guidance in the correspondence with institutions of high repute
  * Identify the hurdles students face in making it to quality internship programmes and suggest remedial measures
  * Establish and maintain collaborations with esteemed organisations where our students can have practical exposure to their disciplines in action


_**Faculty in charge**_
**INTERNSHIP / (SUMMER INTERNSHIP, INTERNSHIP, INTERNSHALA)**
**Coordinator**
**- Dr Jiji Abraham  
- Dr Anamiya Baby  
- Sr Freny Jacob  
- Ms Rinu Thomas  
- Dr Anitha E V**

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Journal Publications
  

  * **The total number of research papers in reputed national and international journals is 128**
  * **The average citation index in Scopus/ Web of Science or PubMed is 2.87**
  * **The h-index of the Institution in Scopus/ Web of Science is 7.5**

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### NCC
  

The NCC unit of Vimala College functioning under Commanding Officer Col Rajinder Singh Sidhu and the 7 Kerala Girls Battalion has been an arena of exuberant activities towards community development, social awareness and character building of the young generation. NCC is not just an organisation but an emotion which makes a cadet lament from her heart, when she completes her tenure of 3 years. But remember, "Once a cadet, is always a cadet!". 
* * *
**MOTTO****--------------------------------------------------------**
NCC Unit of Vimala College is a unit of 52 vibrant cadets who works with one vision and one motto - 'Unity and Discipline’. Working for the betterment of one self as well as people around them, NCC is a sheer feeling of patriotic commitment and encouragement to contribute towards the national development. Even the pandemic couldn’t stop the cadets from doing their part for the society. It's always the selfless attitude and the ever-thriving spirit of the cadets that makes this organisation a humongous success. 
**AIMS****------------------------------------------------------------**
  * To Develop Character, Comradeship, Discipline, Leadership, Secular Outlook, Spirit of Adventure, and Ideals of Selfless Service amongst the Youth of the Country. 
  * To Create a Human Resource of Organized, Trained and Motivated Youth. 
  * To Provide Leadership in all Walks of life and be Always Available for the Service of the Nation. 
  * To Provide a Suitable Environment to Motivate the Youth to Take Up a Career in the Armed Forces.

##  A Legacy of Excellence: Ritorno Presents Star of Vimala 2026 
The Alumnae Association of Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, is proud to announce the 2026 edition of its flagship event, Star of Vimala. This prestigious competition continues a long-standing tradition of celebrating the multifaceted talents of our students. Focused on the core pillars of Talent, Leadership, and Excellence, the event seeks to identify a "star" who embodies the spirit and values of Vimala College. Join us in the College Auditorium as we witness a new name carved into our hall of fame.
  * [ ](http://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=www.vimalacollege.edu.in/news/A-Legacy-of-Excellence--Ritorno-Presents-Star-of-Vimala-2026&t=A%20Legacy%20of%20Excellence:%20Ritorno%20Presents%20Star%20of%20Vimala%202026)

##  Magnus 2k26: Vimala College Celebrates Golden Jubilee with Grand Management Fest 
The PG Department of Commerce and Research at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, successfully hosted Magnus 2k26, a national-level management fest, to commemorate its Golden Jubilee year. This year’s flagship event, themed "Business Track," featured a diverse range of management competitions including the Best Management Team, HR Game, Marketing Game, and Business Quiz. Beyond corporate strategy, the fest also showcased artistic talents through a Non-Management track, highlighting events like Group Dance, Photography, and Reel Making. The event brought together students from various institutions across the country, fostering leadership, creativity, and holistic development in a competitive yet collaborative environment.
  * [ ](http://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=www.vimalacollege.edu.in/news/Magnus-2k26--Vimala-College-Celebrates-Golden-Jubilee-with-Grand-Management-Fest&t=A%20Legacy%20of%20Excellence:%20Ritorno%20Presents%20Star%20of%20Vimala%202026)

##  Global Academic Laurels: Dr. Vanaja V.S. at the 19th World Sanskrit Conference, Nepal 
International Achievement by Faculty (Abroad) - Dr. Vanaja VS has been invited for 19th WORLD SANSKRIT CONFERENCE at Kathmandu, Nepal, a prestigious global Sanskrit gathering from 26th to 30th June 2025 organized by Nepal Sanskrit University with the financial assistance from Central Sanskrit University, New Delhi. Dr. Vanaja VS presented a Research Paper on "Buddha's Perception on Happiness and Peace". - The Jvālā Malayalam translation of the famous Sanskrit drama Dūtaghaṭotkacam of Mahākavi Bhāsa by Dr. Vanaja V. S. and Dr. Reshma Ramachandran P was formally released by Prof. P. C. Muraleemadhavan and presented to Prof. Haridath Sharma on 28th June 2025 in Nepal. Dr. Vanaja VS attended Panel discussion with eminent scholars and researchers in Sanskrit language, literature, philosophy and allied disciplines. She has also visited many world heritage places at Nepal. - Dr. Vanaja VS has been selected as a Member from India in International Association of Sanskrit Studies as part of World Sanskrit Conference
  * [ ](http://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=www.vimalacollege.edu.in/news/Global-Academic-Laurels-Dr.-Vanaja-VS-at-the-19th-World-Sanskrit-Conference--Nepal&t=A%20Legacy%20of%20Excellence:%20Ritorno%20Presents%20Star%20of%20Vimala%202026)

##  Python Training Success at Vimala College 
Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, recently hosted a successful two-day hands-on training program on "Python for Data Analysis" on January 15 and 16, 2026. Organised by the Post Graduate & Research Department of Statistics in collaboration with the UGC Centre for Women’s Studies, the workshop provided participants with practical expertise in modern data tools. Led by AI and ML mentor Namitha R, the sessions covered a comprehensive curriculum including Jupyter notebooks, Pandas for data manipulation, and advanced visualization libraries like Matplotlib and Seaborn. This initiative highlights the department’s commitment to academic excellence following its recent elevation to a Research Department under Calicut University.

##  Vimala College Hosts International Seminar on Biodiversity and Conservation 
On January 7th and 8th, 2026, the Department of Zoology at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in association with the UGC Centre for Women’s Studies and DST Curie, hosted a landmark International Seminar titled "Reversing Extinction, Restoring Hope". The event brought together global experts to discuss critical thrust areas, including biodiversity, environmental biology, and toxicology. +4 The seminar featured prominent speakers such as Dr. K.V. Sankaran, who discussed invasive alien species, and Ingrid Coetzee from ICLEI Africa, who spoke on protecting urban biodiversity. Other sessions covered topics ranging from amphibian extinction to ecosystem conservation for disaster risk reduction. The event also provided a platform for UG/PG students and research scholars to present original research, with selected abstracts included in an ISBN-certified book published by the college.

##  International Seminar on Sustainable Futures: Ecology, Environment and Eco-Tourism 
The Post Graduate and Research Department of Sociology, Vimala College (Autonomous), successfully concluded its One Day International Seminar on "Ecology, Environment and Eco-Tourism in the Modern Context" today, December 16, 2025. The event provided a dynamic platform for critical discussion, featuring distinguished resource persons, including Dr. Fency Sivadasan (Director, Sarawak Tourism Board, Malaysia), Dr. T V Sajeev (Chief Scientist, KFRI), and Mr. K Sethu Raman IPS (Director, Kerala Police Academy). Sessions covered vital topics such as Responsible Tourism, indigenous conservation practices, and reflections on climate change field work. The seminar also hosted parallel paper presentations from research scholars and faculty, reinforcing Vimala College's commitment to fostering international academic exchange and environmental stewardship.

##  Inauguration of Five New Research Centres at Vimala College

##  Seminar on Global Opportunities at the University of Birmingham 
The International Student Cell of Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in association with Michaels Wings Global for International Scholarships, organized a seminar titled “Dream, Apply, Achieve: Opportunities at the University of Birmingham” on 03 December 2025 at Marian Hall. Ms. Anitha Nathan, Country Manager, University of Birmingham, led the session, offering insights into international admissions, scholarships, and pathways for higher education abroad. The seminar provided students with valuable guidance on preparing for global academic opportunities.

##  Aurora Litterarum - Association Inauguration 2025 
PG & Research Department of English Inaugurates 'Aurora Litterarum' Association The PG & Research Department of English, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, successfully inaugurated its Association, Aurora Litterarum – The Dawn of Letters, on November 21, 2025, at 10.30 am in Marian Hall. ##  International Seminar on Degrading Ecosystems: Challenges, Consequences and Conservation Strategies 
The Department of Botany, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in collaboration with St. Joseph’s College (Autonomous), Tiruchirappalli, and funded by the Kerala State Biodiversity Board, is organizing an International Seminar on “Degrading Ecosystems: Challenges, Consequences, and Conservation Strategies” on 19th and 20th November 2025 at Marian Hall. The event aims to explore critical environmental issues through discussions on biodiversity conservation, sustainable development, and ecosystem restoration. Researchers, scholars, and students are invited to present papers and participate in this two-day seminar.

##  Talk on Cyber Crimes: Myths and Realities 
The Postgraduate Departments of Sociology and Statistics at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, jointly organized a talk on “Cyber Crimes: Myths and Realities” on November 7, 2025, at Marian Hall. The session featured Mr. Hariprasad M. K., Assistant Commandant of Police, IRB Thrissur, as the resource person. ##  Workshop on Computerized Psychological Testing Using PEBL 
The Postgraduate Department of Psychology, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, organized a workshop on Computerized Test – PEBL (Psychology Experiment Building Language) on October 30, 2025, from 9:30 a.m. to 10:30 a.m. The session was conducted in the Multimedia Room of the college, under the guidance of the Head of the Department, Dr. Sinto P. Anto, and with the support of the Principal, Dr. Sr. Beena Jose. The resource persons for the workshop were Ms. Aparna Suresh and Ms. Deva Reghuchandran Nair, Assistant Professors in the Department of Psychology. The session provided students with hands-on exposure to computerized psychological testing using PEBL, an open-source platform widely used for cognitive and experimental assessments.

Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, observed World Suicide Prevention Day on September 10, 2025, at 12 PM in the basketball court. ##  Revitalizing Kerala Economy: Issues & Challenges - Day 2 
The second day of the Two-Day National Conference on “Revitalizing Kerala Economy: Issues & Challenges” organized by the PG & Research Department of Economics, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in association with South Indian Bank, witnessed insightful discussions on crucial economic themes. The day began with a technical session led by Dr. Madhura Swaminathan, Professor, Indian Statistical Institute, Bangalore, on “Food and Nutritional Security: Kerala's Achievements and Challenges.” Following this, research scholars presented their papers in two parallel venues, showcasing diverse perspectives on Kerala’s economic resilience. A panel discussion on “Fiscal Crisis in Kerala: Issues and Challenges” brought together eminent economists including Dr. Lakshmi Devi, Sri. Emmanuel Thomas Kanatt, Dr. K. P. Vipin Chandran, and Dr. Rajesh K, moderated by Dr. Chacko Jose P. The conference concluded with a Valedictory Session graced by Sri. Radhakrishnan E, Thrissur Regional Head, South Indian Bank, followed by the prize distribution of the National Level Essay Competition and certificate distribution to participants. The two-day conference provided a rich platform for dialogue, research sharing, and critical policy discussions aimed at addressing Kerala’s economic challenges.

##  Mehfil-e-Qawwali - A Celebration of Indias Musical & Linguistic Heritage 
The Department of English, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in association with the UGC Centre for Women’s Studies, Saarang Music Club, Department of Hindi, and the English Alumnae Association, organized Mehfil-e-Qawwali on 22nd August 2025. The event recreated the traditional mehfil atmosphere where renowned vocalists and musicians brought alive the essence of Sufi music through poetry woven in Hindi, Urdu, Punjabi, Persian, and other languages. The soulful performances explored themes of love, devotion, longing, and spiritual ecstasy, offering the audience a unique cultural and spiritual experience.

##  Vimala College Students Forum Takes Up the Cause of Tribal Communities 
Vimala College is proud to announce that students from the college are actively engaging with the cause of tribal communities. The forum's members will visit tribal communities to gain a firsthand understanding of their challenges and work towards finding sustainable solutions.

##  Two-Day National Conference on Revitalizing Kerala Economy: Issues & Challenges - Day 1 
The PG & Research Department of Economics, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in association with South Indian Bank, inaugurated the Two-Day National Conference on "Revitalizing Kerala Economy: Issues & Challenges" on 21st August 2025. The inaugural session featured an address by Prof. (Dr.) B. A. Prakash, leading economist and former Head, Department of Economics, University of Kerala, and a keynote address by Sri. Anto George T, COO of South Indian Bank. The event also included felicitation by eminent academicians from the University of Calicut. The day comprised three insightful technical sessions: Prof. (Dr.) B. A. Prakash discussed Critical Development Issues of Kerala Economy: Strategy and Policies. Dr. V. K. Vijayakumar, Chief Investment Strategist at Geojit Investments, explored Kerala’s Crisis: Fiscal or Economic? Dr. Priyesh C.A, Government College Attingal, addressed Fiscal Crisis and Issues. The day concluded with engaging discussions, feedback sessions, and participant interactions, setting the stage for further deliberations on Day 2.

##  Socratic Colloquium on Why Liberty 
The Department of Sociology, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in association with South Asia Students for Liberty (SFL), organized a Socratic Colloquium based on the book “Why Liberty” on 20th August 2025 at Marian Hall. The session was led by Shri Amal Chandra (Author, Policy Analyst, Columnist, and Coordinator, SFL India), who engaged students in an interactive discussion on the themes of liberty, personal choices, and future responsibilities. The program commenced with a welcome address by Dr. Binu K. (Head, Department of Sociology), followed by the Presidential Address by Dr. Sr. Beena Jose (Principal). The event concluded with a vote of thanks delivered by Dr. Manjima M. V. (Faculty Coordinator).

##  Manahsastram 2025 - Grand Psychology Fest at Vimala College 
The PG Department of Psychology, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, hosted Manahsastram 2025 from 11th to 13th August 2025 under the theme “Psychology in Action: Building Mentally Healthy Communities.” The three-day fest featured a National Seminar, paper presentations, and a variety of creative competitions including fashion shows, dance, quizzes, poster making, and games. Eminent psychologists and academicians such as Dr. Linjo C J, Dr. Leemamol Mathew, Dr. Sunny Joseph, Dr. Raheemudheen P K, Dr. Bayana BOMS, and Dr. Justine K James shared their expertise on resilience, mental health, and community well-being. The event successfully combined academic insight with creativity, fostering awareness and dialogue on mental wellness.

##  Inauguration of Vimala Bodhika - Centre for Indian Knowledge Systems 
Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur proudly inaugurated Vimala Bodhika – Centre for Indian Knowledge Systems (IKS Centre) on 8th August 2025 at the college auditorium. The event was graced by Padma Bhushan Dr. Mallika Sarabhai, Chancellor of Kerala Kalamandalam, as the Chief Guest. The felicitation address was delivered by Dr. P. Rajesh Kumar, Registrar of Kerala Kalamandalam. This historic initiative aims to promote, preserve, and disseminate the rich heritage of Indian knowledge systems, encouraging students and scholars to explore interdisciplinary domains rooted in tradition and culture.

##  Xylia 2025: A Celebration of Nature and Knowledge at Vimala College 
Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, successfully hosted "Xylia 2025," a Zoology Fest on August 5, 2025. Organized in collaboration with several esteemed institutions, the event was a vibrant celebration of nature, knowledge, and innovation. The fest featured a wide array of activities, including engaging competitions like the "Grey Matter" Quiz, Clay Modelling, Photography, and Poster Making, all with cash prizes. Attendees also enjoyed diverse attractions such as exhibition stalls showcasing everything from Ayurvedic practices to indigenous dairy products, a documentary screening, interactive spot games, and a "Zoology-Clinic" offering services like BMI and blood pressure checks. The festival provided students, faculty, and visitors with a day of learning, creativity, and fun, fostering a deeper appreciation for the wonders of the natural world.

##  Life Saving Skills: CPR and First Aid Training 
The Department of Home Science, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in collaboration with Amala College of Nursing, conducted a hands-on training session on “Life Saving Skills: CPR and First Aid” on 31st July 2025. The session, held as part of an MoU activity, was led by Mrs. Fasna Jabar A, Lecturer, Amala College of Nursing. Students were trained on emergency response techniques essential for real-life situations.

##  STAT SYMPHO 2025: National Statistics Day Celebrations at Vimala 
As part of the 19th National Statistics Day Celebrations, the PG Department of Statistics, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, organized STAT SYMPHO on 30th July 2025. The event featured a thought-provoking talk on “Statistics in Daily Life” by Dr. Rani Sebastian, Assistant Professor at St. Thomas College (Autonomous), Thrissur, held at SJ Hall. The celebrations also included an All Kerala High School Level Quiz Competition, conducted at 8:30 AM in the Silver Jubilee Seminar Hall, which witnessed enthusiastic participation from across the state. The event highlighted the importance of statistical literacy and its applications in everyday life.

##  The Uncelebrated Wanderers: Report Release & Documentary Screening on Keralas Golden Jackal 
The Department of Zoology, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in collaboration with Aranyakam Nature Foundation, organized the official release of the report titled "The Uncelebrated Wanderers". The event, held on 28th July 2025 at Lissieux Hall, featured a detailed presentation on the distribution and ecology of Canis aureus (Golden Jackal) in Kerala based on Citizen Science inputs. The program also included the screening of the award-winning documentary "Follow the Howl: Jackal – The Real Story" by Abhijith Perambra, providing participants with profound insights into wildlife conservation.

##  Empowering Communities through Nutrition: A Career Perspective 
The Department of home science in association with UGC centre for Women's Studies organized a talk on Empowering Communities through Nutrition : A career perspective. The session was handled by Mrs Cindin John, Clinical and Sports Nutritionist, Atreya hospital, Thrissur and an alumna of the home science department. ##  Kargil Vijay Diwas Observed with Honour at Vimala College 
The National Cadet Corps (7 Kerala Girls Battalion) of Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, solemnly observed Kargil Vijay Diwas on 25th July 2025 at the College Auditorium. The event, titled "Sindoor Ki Saugandh", was graced by Col. H Padmanabhan (Retd.), a Kargil War Veteran, and Smt. Jalaja Viswanathan (Veer Nari) as Guests of Honour. A heartfelt floral tribute was offered at Smrithi Mandap, followed by an inspiring session honouring the valor and sacrifice of the Indian Army. The ceremony aimed to instill patriotic fervour and gratitude among the students and staff, coordinated by Capt. Dr. Lovji K N (ANO), JUO Christeen M L, and the NCC team.

##  Graduation Ceremony 2025 - A Celebration of Academic Excellence 
Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, held its prestigious Graduation Ceremony 2025 on 14th July, honouring graduates for their academic achievements and holistic development. The event was inaugurated by Prof Dr Denoj Sebastian, Registrar, University of Calicut. In his keynote address, he emphasized the importance of research and innovation for a sustainable future. The convocation ceremony, held in the presence of Ms Sheeba Mumtaz C K, Assistant Director, Department of Social Justice, Govt of Kerala, Dr Jos T Puthur, Professor, Dept of Botany, Director of IQAC, University of Calicut, Sr Saly Paul, Provincial Superior and Manager, CMC Nirmala Province, Thrissur, Principal Dr. Sr. Beena Jose, Dr Vimala M, Controller of Examinations; saw enthusiastic participation from graduates, faculty, and parents, making it a proud and memorable day for all.

##  World Jackfruit Day Celebrated with Culinary Flair 
The Department of Home Science at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in collaboration with Malayala Manorama and Ayur Jack Farm, celebrated World Jackfruit Day on 4th July 2025 at the Silver Jubilee Hall. The event featured a recipe competition and demonstration class, emphasizing the culinary and nutritional significance of jackfruit. Mr. A Jeevankumar (Coordinating Editor, Malayala Manorama) delivered the presidential address, and Mr. Varghese Tharakan (Owner, Ayurjack Farm) served as the chief guest. Dr. Sr. Beena Jose, Principal, inaugurated the event, which brought together students, faculty, and food enthusiasts in a delightful tribute to the versatile fruit.

##  Life Science Summit 2025 - Day 1: Echoes of Life Begins with Insightful Inauguration and Expert Talks 
The Department of Zoology, in collaboration with the Bhoomithrasena Club, inaugurated the Life Science Summit 2025–26 on 24th June 2025 at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur. Day 1 commenced with an official inauguration followed by enlightening sessions by Dr. V. S. Vijayan (Chairman, Salim Ali Foundation) on Biodiversity Conservation and Sustainable Development and Dr. Lalitha Vijayan (Honorary Director, Salim Ali Foundation) who presented a case study on Vellangallur Panchayat as a Model for Sustainable Development. The day concluded with vibrant paper presentations by students and researchers on diverse themes in life sciences.

##  Life Science Summit 2025 - Day 2: Marine Biodiversity, Biospheres and Student Research Take the Spotlight 
The second day of the Life Science Summit 2025–26, held on 25th June 2025, at Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, brought engaging sessions and research to the forefront. Dr. Jayaraj K. A. (Associate Professor, Pondicherry University, Andaman Campus) delivered a compelling talk on Marine Biodiversity Threats and Conservation, followed by Mrs. Manjusha U. on Closed Biosphere Terrariums. ##  Vimala College Shines in India Today - MDRA Best College Survey 2025 
Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, has once again made its mark nationally in the India Today – MDRA Best College Survey 2025. The college secured impressive ranks in multiple streams: 61st in Arts 41st in Science 40th in Commerce 26th in MSW (Master of Social Work) Notably, the MSW programme has also been ranked 8th in India under the category of Best Value for Money Programmes, highlighting the college's commitment to accessible, quality education. These rankings are a testament to Vimala’s academic excellence, student-focused initiatives, and holistic development.

##  Vimala College Celebrates International Yoga Day 2025 
In observance of International Yoga Day 2025, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, organized a peaceful and energizing yoga session under the guidance of the Department of Physical Education. Students from across departments gathered in unity to perform Surya Namaskaram (Sun Salutation) and Chandra Namaskaram (Moon Salutation), embracing the essence of yoga as a path to holistic wellness. ##  Awareness Session on Drug Abuse and Social Responsibility 
An awareness session on “Drug Abuse, Empathy and Social Responsibility” organised by the Post Graduate Department of Commerce and Research for the second year Bcom students was conducted by Shri Jose C.C, trustee of TRE Palliative Care, Cheroor, Thrissur, on the 13th of June 2025 as part of the observance of the International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking. Known for his unique style “Pattum Parachilum”, he engaged students through songs and moving stories that highlighted the impact of drug abuse and the importance of empathy. Students also contributed essential items like soaps and sanitary napkins for POCSO victims, reinforcing the session’s message of compassion and social responsibility.

##  Beautification Project Launched under Garbage-Free Kerala Initiative 
Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, in collaboration with NSS Units 43 & 120, officially launched the “Beautification of College Road” under the ‘Garbage-Free Kerala’ Initiative on June 5, 2025. The inaugural ceremony was graced by Hon. Mayor Sri M. K. Varghese, along with other dignitaries and civic leaders. ##  National Level Essay Contest on Kerala's Economic Crisis 
The Post Graduate Department of Economics, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, organized a National Level Essay Contest sponsored by South Indian Bank, Thrissur, focusing on Kerala’s ongoing economic crisis. Despite receiving 18 entries from students across India, the jury found none met the required conceptual and analytical standards. As a result, no top prizes were awarded; instead, three participants - Ardra K Baburaj (Vimala College), Nidhi S. (CUSAT), and Bonny John (Symbiosis University) - were recognized with consolation prizes of ₹10,000 each for their efforts.

##  Rank Lists for Guest Lecturer Appointments - Academic Year 2025-2026 
Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, has officially published the rank lists of selected candidates for Guest Lecturer positions across various departments including Malayalam, Hindi, Sanskrit, Political Science, Sociology, Commerce, Social Work, Mathematics, Computer Science, Zoology and Home Science. The interviews were held on 22nd and 23rd May 2025. The college congratulates all shortlisted candidates and extends best wishes as they contribute to academic excellence in the upcoming academic year.

##  Book Release: Innovative Research Advances in Commerce and Management 
PG Department of Commerce & Research of Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur has released a book 'Innovative Research Advances in Commerce and Management'. Dr Salini K, Assistant Professor and Research Guide of the PG Department of Commerce and Research, edited the book. The release was done by Sr Tesseena P. Emmatty, Vice Principal, and Dr Preema Rose Nichlavose, Head of the Department, in the presence of all faculty members and PG students of the department on 28/02/2025 at noon at Carmel Conference Hall, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur. The book contains valuable contributions by the students and faculty of Vimala College and other colleges.
  * [ ](http://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=www.vimalacollege.edu.in/news/Book-Release:-Innovative-Research-Advances-in-Commerce-and-Management&t=A%20Legacy%20of%20Excellence:%20Ritorno%20Presents%20Star%20of%20Vimala%202026)

##  Field Visit to Kerala Fire & Rescue Services Academy 
The PG Department of Sociology, in association with the Fire Force Academy, organized a field visit to the Kerala Fire and Rescue Services Academy, Thrissur, as part of the Disaster Management multidisciplinary course. Conducted by Officers Radhakrishnan, Aswin, and Vishnu, the session provided valuable insights into disaster preparedness, emergency response, and rescue operations. Students participated in hands-on training, including CPR and first aid demonstrations, enhancing their confidence in handling emergency situations. The visit emphasized the importance of teamwork, coordination, and communication in disaster management. This enriching experience highlighted the need for disaster awareness and preparedness, paving the way for future collaborations with the Fire Force Academy.
  * [ ](http://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=www.vimalacollege.edu.in/news/Field-Visit-to-Kerala-Fire-&-Rescue-Services-Academy&t=A%20Legacy%20of%20Excellence:%20Ritorno%20Presents%20Star%20of%20Vimala%202026)

##  Field Visit to Chettuva Harbor Settlement 
The PG Department of Sociology, Vimala College (Autonomous), Thrissur, successfully visited Chettuva Harbor to understand the socio-economic issues and the problems of mining. 35 students and 2 faculty members visit the place to explore different realities of the problem.
  * [ ](http://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=www.vimalacollege.edu.in/news/Field-Visit-to-Chettuva-Harbor-Settlement&t=A%20Legacy%20of%20Excellence:%20Ritorno%20Presents%20Star%20of%20Vimala%202026)

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### NSS
  

Aimed at developing student's personality through community service, NSS is a voluntary association of young people in Vimala College.
**_Objectives_**
  * To understand the community in which they work
  * To understand themselves in relation to their community
  * To identify the needs and problems of the community and involve them in problem-solving
  * To develop among themselves a sense of social and civic responsibility
  * To utilise their knowledge in finding practical solutions to individual and community problems
  * To develop competence required for group-living and sharing of responsibilities
  * To gain skills in mobilising community participation
  * To acquire leadership qualities and democratic attitudes
  * To develop capacity to meet emergencies and natural disasters and
  * To practise national integration and social harmony


**_Motto:_**
The Motto of NSS "Not Me But You", reflects the essence of democratic living and upholds the need for self-less service. NSS helps the students develop appreciation to other person's point of view and also show consideration to '/other living beings. The philosophy of the NSS is well doctrined in this motto, which underlines/on the belief that the welfare .of an individual is ultimately dependent on the welfare of the society on the whole and therefore, the NSS volunteers shall strive for the well-being of the society.
**_Symbol:_**
The symbol for the NSS has been based on the giant Rath Wheel of the world famous Konark Sun Temple (The Black Pagoda) situated in Orissa, India. The wheel portrays the cycle of creation, preservation and release and signifies the movement in life across time and space, The symbol thus stands for continuity as well as change and implies the continuous striving of NSS for social change.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Objectives
  

  * Create quality awareness and consciousness amongst all stakeholders
  * Formulate strategies for curriculum enrichment
  * Develop quality benchmarks for various academic and administrative activities of the College
  * Conduct periodical academic and administrative auditing
  * Facilitate learner-centric environment conducive to quality education
  * Expedite student support services
  * Enhance use of ICT in teaching- learning and governance
  * Promote scientific research skills among faculty and students
  * Develop and administer feedback mechanism and respond appropriately to fulfill stakeholder expectations
  * Organise continuous professional development programmes for faculty and administrative staff
  * Initiate innovative curricular, co-curricular and extracurricular activities and institutionalize best practices
  * Establish collaboration and networking with other institutions

#### Principal's Desk
  

![](https://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/Principal%20Photo\(1\).png)
**Dr. Sr. Beena Jose, Principal**
####    
Principal's Message
![](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/image/under-line_3.png)
Many are Called, but only a few are chosen, says the Scripture. This academic year, the 58th year of this acclaimed institution arrived with new views, visions, ventures and endeavours with the COVID 19 Pandemic outbreak. Online collaborative systems and virtual classrooms replaced real classrooms with thier flexible mode of teaching, knowledge retention and increased engagement.
The realm of higher education always instill us with the question of enhancing and updating the skills of main stakeholders with the intention to capture new worthy spheres and to connect them with the community. Campus to community is the thrust area which is envisaged in promoting novel ideologies in a practical way. This thought has been executed effectively in many ways like organizing awareness programes for the community, promoting financial aid ventures, environmental related projects, internships, response to COVID 19 Pandemic etc.
Dear Vimalites, this is exactly what I hope for you. Your days here should equip you with all the skills that one needs to contribute to holistic development of an individual. As the butterfly comes out of the chrysalis, let all of you be adorned with flying colours.
May the thousands of smiles that have illumined the corridors of Vimala prove to be a solace to the world like tiny droplets on parched earth.
Best Wishes & God Bless you
####    
Principal's Profile  
![](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/image/under-line_3.png)
Dr Sr Beena Jose, the young principal of Vimala college is an erudite scholar with manifold academic achievements: research guideship, publications in refereed international journals, overseas paper presentations, international visiting scholar at Scholarship and Christianity in Oxford(SCIO), United Kingdom, minor and major research projects including, postdoctoral fellowship at Santa Clara University, California, TORCH fellowship from Ian Ramsey Centre, University of Oxford, membership in APCBEES, and so forth. She has been adorned with several awards like the Shyama Prasad Mukherjee Fellowship and the Kanakam Thampuran Memorial Prize and GRABS Achievement Award for Excellence in Teaching and Research in 2019[...Read more](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/departments/chemistry/faculty/MTA0)
**[Academic Visitor -](http://irc.web.ox.ac.uk/people/beena-jose)[ University of Oxford](http://irc.web.ox.ac.uk/people/beena-jose)![](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/1\(24\).gif)**
![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/images/foo-icon1.png)

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Principal's Desk
  

![](https://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/ALW05747--1\(3\).jpg)
**Dr. Sr. Beena Jose, Principal**
####    
Principal's Message
![](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/image/under-line_3.png)
Many are Called, but only a few are chosen, says the Scripture. This academic year, the 58th year of this acclaimed institution arrived with new views, visions ventures and endavours.
The realm of higher education with the implementation of the Four year Under Graduate programme marks a significant paradigm shift in Indian higher education. Moving away from rigid, discipline-specific curricula, FYUGP embraces flexibility, allowing students to customize their learning paths by choosing subjects from various streams. It emphasizes holistic development through the inclusion of vocational education, life skills and extracurricular activities. A key feature is the multiple exit options, enabling students to earn a certificate, diploma, or degree based on their progression, reducing the pressure of completing a traditional four-year degree. The program also fosters innovation through research-focused learning and promotes credit mobility across institutions, making education more adaptable, student-centric, and aligned with the needs of a dynamic job market.
Dear Vimalites, Just as a river carves its path through the mountains, so too does your journey here at college shape the course of your future. Every challenge you face is like a stone in the river, refining your character and strength. Flow with determination, for the direction you set today will lead you to the vast ocean of success.
Keep moving forward, steady and strong.
Best Wishes & God Bless you
####    
Principal's Profile  
![](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/image/under-line_3.png)
Dr Sr Beena Jose, the principal of Vimala college is an erudite scholar with manifold academic achievements: research guideship, publications in refereed international journals, overseas paper presentations, international visiting scholar at Scholarship and Christianity in Oxford(SCIO), United Kingdom, minor and major research projects including, postdoctoral fellowship at Santa Clara University, California, TORCH fellowship from Ian Ramsey Centre, University of Oxford, membership in APCBEES, and so forth. She has been adorned with several awards like the Shyama Prasad Mukherjee Fellowship, Kanakam Thampuran Memorial Prize, GRABS Achievement Award for Excellence in Teaching and Research in 2019 and the prestigious Prof. M.M.Ghani Award for the Best College Teacher in Science Category (University of Calicut) in 2023 [...Read more](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/departments/chemistry/faculty/MTA0)
**[Academic Visitor -](http://irc.web.ox.ac.uk/people/beena-jose)[ University of Oxford](http://irc.web.ox.ac.uk/people/beena-jose) ![](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/1\(24\).gif)**
![](https://www.vimalacollege.edu.in/images/foo-icon1.png)

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Profile of the College
  
**Name of the College** | Vimala College (Autonomous)  
---|---  
**Place** | Thrissur  
**State** | Kerala  
**Affiliating University** | University of Calicut  
**Status of the College** | Autonomous  
**Financial Category** | Grant-in-aid and Self-Financing  
**No.of Departments** | 20 (15 Departments offering regular programmes and 5 ancillary Departments)  
**No.of Programmes** | UG: 19 PG:16 PhD: 6  
**Other programmes** | 27  
**Year of Establishment** | 1967  
**UGC recognition** | Autonomous  
**Location of the College** | Urban  
**Area of the Campus** | 29.6 Acres

#### Programme Outcomes
  

**Programme outcome : PG**
**At the end of the PG programme, students will have (been)**
  1. Acquired core knowledge, both enduring and contemporary.
  2. Cultivated an aptitude for research and passion for lifelong learning.
  3. Sufficiently Informed to respond pragmatically and judiciously to current developments / issues - be it local, regional, national or global.
  4. Internalized the ability to source and utilize information.
  5. Moulded for the spontaneous expression of creativity to connect, modify, expand and develop.
  6. Acquired the expertise to apply knowledge for environmental sustenance.
  7. Incorporated disciplinary, interdisciplinary and supradisciplinary fields of study to understand, analyze and solve problems.
  8. Equipped with the competencies to respond to one’s calling, be it employment, entrepreneurship, self engagement, service, or any other.
  9. Procured empowerment for self enhancement and betterment of others, for common good and social welfare.
  10. Discerned the precepts of professionalism and professional ethics


**Programme outcome : UG**
**At the end of the UG programme, students will have (been)**
  1. Acquired good subject knowledge.
  2. Cultivated intellectual curiosity and love for learning.
  3. Acquired a sound foundation for student progression.
  4. Comprehended the nuances of research and research ethics.
  5. Obtained fluency in language and ability for effective communication.
  6. Obtained problem solving and analytical skills.
  7. Explored multidisciplinary domains for proficiency (communication, dependability, team work).
  8. Imbibed sound values and principles.
  9. Explored knowledge and learning for environment sustenance.
  10. Ability to collaborate for common good and social welfare.


**ADDITIONAL INSTITUTIONAL OUTCOMES**
At the end of the programme a Vimalite will have
  * Comprehended the rights and responsibilities of good citizenship
  * Acquired life skills, values and competence to meet the demands and challenges of everyday life.
  * Demonstrated the employment of various literacies like textual, literary, scientific, artistic, numerical, technological.
  * Explored the path of self discovery for the expression of one’s own talents, passion and dedication
  * Demonstrated logical decision making and prudent reasoning when challenged with ethical problems

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Grievance Redressal Cell
  

Vimala College provides an adequate platform for students for redressing their grievances on academic and non academic matters. An aggrieved student may appear either in person or be represented by such person as may be authorized to present her case.The aggrieved student may approach any member of Cell directly or through the Tutor. The Cell shall be guided by the principles of natural justice.The Cell shall ensure the disposal of every application as early as possibleas and not later than a month of the receipt of grievance. Grievances regarding administration, teaching, learning, results and conduct of examinations, valuation, and so forth are dealt with by this cell.
**Committe Members**

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### RESEARCH
  

**Vimala College Autonomous Thrissur has six research centres – English, Commerce, Physics, Social Work,Economics and Malayalam. The institution strictly adheres to the rules and regulations of the University of Calicut, besides framing a Research Policy of its own.**
** POLICIES FOR RESEARCH PROMOTION**
Vimala College has an adequate research policy to foster the research in an intensive and collaboratine manner. Vimala College's culture of collaboration drives innovative contributions vital to the sustance of the contemporary scenario. To compliment teaching and learning processes the institution provides a platform to carry out research with freedom of enquiry, thought, expression and publication.
[**Research Policy**](http://vimalacollege.edu.in/uploads_new/userfiles/Research.pdf)
**COMMITTEES FOR RESEARCH PROMOTION**
The following committees work in collaboration for the promotion of the research in the instituiton.
  * **Research and Consultancy Cell**


The objectives of the cell inlcudes the drafting of the Research Policy and the inculcation of the research culture among the staff and students. The following are the members of the committe:-
Principal - Dr Sr Beena Jose
Vice Principal - Dr Malini K
Coordinator - Dr Veena Gopalan E
- Dr Jaya Cherian
Joint Coordinator & Research Nodal Officer - Dr Preema Rose Nichlavose 
IQAC Coordinator - Dr Dhanya Johnson
Members - Dr Honey Sebastian
- Dr Salini K 
- Dr Nisha Francis O 
- Dr Jerin Paul 
- Dr Bijoy P Mathew 
- Dr Mallika Nair
- Mr Sojan Joseph 
- Dr Bindhu K Jose
  * **Research Ethics Committe**


Principal - Dr Sr Beena Jose
Coordinator  - Dr Treesa T Pulickal
- Dr Binu K
- Dr Bindu Ann Philip
- Dr Honey Sebastian
Concerened Research Supervisor
  * **Research Advisory Committe**


The admission at Research centres of the instituiton shall consists of Principal, three experts, one person nominated by the Vice-Chancellor, supervising teacher of the research scholar concerned, Head of the department, if he/she has Ph.D. The Ph D admission and progress evaluation of the students are carried by the RAC committe of each research student. The minutes of the RAC meetings are recorded.
  * **Intellectual Property Rights****Cell**


Intellectual Property Rights have played a crucial role in assuring the legal protection of our creativity as well as taking it to the next level of global exposure via collaborations with industrial, academic or publishing firms. While promoting the dissemination of knowledge without the fear of losing our claim on it, it also ensures economic incentives to the creators of innovative ideas. The following are the objectives of IPR
  1. To familiarize students, researchers and faculty with various steps to be taken in their pursuits of securing their inventions and innovations
  2. To acquaint the participants with management of Intellectual Property Rights
  3. To inspire future researchers to make use of the privileges ensured by law in securing their rights on their intellectual property


Coordinator - Dr Honey sebastian
Member - Ms Anuja 
  * **Institution Innovation Cell**

#### Vision
We envision the total transformation of the young women for their enrichment and of the society at large and the nation as a whole.
#### Mission
We dedicate ourselves to the mission of training women for their academic excellence, development of human skill and character formation based on the love of God and service to society and to the Nation.

#### Right to Information
  

#### **Right to Information (Statutory Declaration Under Sec-4(1)(B) of RTI Act 2005)**
Citizens can seek information regarding the activities of the college by submitting a written request with details like Name, address, contact telephone number and particulars of the information sought. The reason for seeking information need not be given. The duly signed request may be addressed to the Public Information Officer, Vimala College (Autonomous), Ramavarmapuram Road, Thrissur 09, Kerala, India along with the required fee of Rs.10/-. Copies of documents will be charged according to the rate fixed by the State Information Commission, Kerala.
RTI officers in-charge
Appellate Authority |  Dr Sr Beena Jose, Principal (mail@vimalacollege.edu.in)  
---|---  
Public Information Officer |  Mr Santhosh P Jose, Assistant Professor, (spjsanthosh@vimalacollege.edu.in)  
Assistant Public Information Officer |  Dr Binu K (binuk@vimalacollege.edu.in)
